text: Recent studies of clustering in light nuclei with an initial energy above 1 AGeV in nuclear treack emulsion are overviewed. The results of investigations ofthe relativistic $^9$Be nuclei fragmentation in emulsion, which entails theproduction of He fragments, are presented. It is shown that most preciseangular measurements provided by this technique play a crucial role in therestoration of the excitation spectrum of the $\alpha$ particle sysytem. Inperipheral interactions $^9$Be nuclei are dissociated practically totallythrough the 0$^+$ and 2$^+$ states of the $^8$Be nucleus.  The results of investigations of the dissociation of a $^{14}$N nucleus ofmomentum 2.86 A GeV/c in emulsion are presented as example of more complicatedsystem. The momentum and correlation characteristics of $\alpha$ particles forthe $^{14}$N$\to3\alpha+X$ channel in the laboratory system and the restsystems of 3$\alpha$ particles were considered in detail.  Topology of charged fragments produced in peripheral relativisticdissociation of radioactive $^8$B, $^7$Be nuclei in emulsion is studied.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review in a human style:"We were really looking forward to our stay at this hotel, especially after reading all the great reviews about the friendly staff. And sure enough, the staff were indeed very friendly and helpful throughout our stay. However, our excitement quickly turned to disappointment when we entered our room and saw the state of the bathroom. It was clear that it hadn't been properly cleaned in a while, with streaks of dirt and grime all over the sink and toilet.But the real kicker was when my wife lifted the slip around the mattress and found that it was stained, old, and smelled terrible. It was clear that the mattress had seen better days and didn't look like it had been changed in a long time. We were both pretty grossed out and felt like we were taking a risk just sleeping on it.Despite the friendly staff, we couldn't overlook the state of the room and bathroom. It was a real shame because we had been looking forward to a comfortable and relaxing stay. Unfortunately, we would not recommend this hotel to anyone, especially not in its current state. We hope they take steps to improve their cleanliness and maintenance standards, because they have the potential to be a great hotel."

If it's lunch.. . nnORDER A PRETZEL ROLL.. . or maybe 3! (They're free with dinner, or at lunch they're $1. ) Crunchy exterior (but not hard) sprinkled with sea salt and served with pats of butter. nnCan I admit, I wish I hadn't offered my husband half of mine, so I could have eaten the whole thing? Silly Dion. nnBut besides that, Waldenhorn's food is authentic German, set in a comfortable, homey setting with friendly servers. Lots of beers on tap, served by the liter and half liter. nnLunch is usually served until 3pm, but on Saturdays it's 4pm. I had the special of the day, the Rindsrouladen, which is braised beef rolled with cabbage, onions, pickle and bacon. It was served with spatzel and green beans. nnI ate the entire thing! The meat is so tender, the spatzel nice and buttery (their spatzel is more like long thin noodles, not little pellet shapes BTW) and even the green beans were nice and crisp and garlicky. No mushy beans here! nnMy husband got the weinershnitzel, and it was pounded perfectly thin and uniform, crisp on the outside and tender and moist on the inside. The fries are the thin, super crispy kind (Think BK.. . but better. Strange comparison, I know. ) and more of those yummy crisp green beans. He too ate it all! nnOur server was very good at explaining each menu item to us, and didn't steer us wrong with her recommendations. She even came by to ask if we wanted more gravy.. . which we did, and could have sopped up 5 pretzel rolls with the stuff! nnPrices were reasonable. Sandwiches about $7-$9 and the lunch entrees about $10 to $11. nnCan't wait to go back and try dinner (Complete with accordion player!? ) or their brunch buffet. Oktoberfest is coming up too, which includes live music and festivities.

And here it goes, from the many other Kohls that I've stepped foot on, this is the most disorganized department store I've been to. nnThe prices are higher than other Kohls' for the EXACT same product. I purchased a cardigan for $21 at the Charlotte Kohls while the day after I visited my hometown Kohls and found the exact cardigan for $15. That was a return. nnThe selection in this Kohls is so poor. I asked an associate if she could help me find a pencil skirt in the middle of the summer and she said that they don't carry skirts. How can they not carry skirts?! ? ! nnLast week I stopped by to get the battery of my watch changed. I purchased this watch for $250 at their store and when I asked the lady to change my battery she told me that she is afraid to do it because she broke her watch changing the battery. Are you kidding me? You also have to pay for a battery change while other Kohls change it for free. nnI love Kohls but everytime I've been to this store I've gotten an attitude from most of the associates and never been able to find a good deal so I've stopped going.

The devil, a devout Catholic, attended mass every Sunday, dressed in his finest suit and tie. He would sit in the front pew, his eyes closed, his hands clasped together in prayer. He would listen intently to the sermon, nodding his head in agreement, and taking notes in a small notebook he kept in his pocket.Despite his reputation as the lord of evil, the devil was a model member of the congregation. He volunteered for every church function, from organizing the annual festival to leading the youth group. He even sang in the choir, his deep, rumbling voice a perfect fit for the bass section.But the devil's faith was not just a show. He truly believed in God and the teachings of the Catholic Church. He believed that by following the teachings of Jesus, he could become a better person, a person worthy of redemption.However, the devil's fellow parishioners were not so sure. They whispered among themselves, wondering how a being with such a reputation for evil could possibly be a true believer. Some even refused to take communion from him, fearing that he might taint the sacred bread and wine.Despite their doubts, the devil persevered. He prayed daily, read scripture, and even fasted during lent. He wanted to be a good Catholic, a worthy servant of God.One day, the priest called the devil into his office. "I have been noticing your devotion to the church," the priest said. "Your faith is inspiring. I believe you are truly trying to change your ways."The devil smiled, feeling a sense of pride and accomplishment. "Thank you, father. I am doing my best to follow God's teachings."The priest nodded. "I believe you are. And I believe that with time, you will find redemption. You will find peace in the love of God."The devil left the priest's office feeling hopeful, feeling that he was finally on the path to redemption. He continued to attend mass, to pray and fast, and to serve the church in any way he could.

We investigate the generation of local de Broglie-Bohm trajectories from entangled wavefunctions in quantum systems. By considering a composite system composed of two entangled subsystems, we demonstrate how the individual trajectories of the subsystems can be obtained using the de Broglie-Bohm pilot-wave formulation. We examine the dynamics of the entangled system under different measurement scenarios and show that the trajectories of the subsystems exhibit nonlocal correlations and depend on the local measurements performed on the particles. Moreover, we analyze the relationship between the entanglement properties of the wavefunction and the behavior of the corresponding de Broglie-Bohm trajectories, providing insights into the nature of quantum entanglement and its connection to the pilot-wave interpretation.

I can try to lay in bed all night, but I know I won' t be getting to sleep anytime soon. The silence permeates into the house, there are no crickets, no wind, no rustling of branches, dead silence. How fitting. Then begins the whispers, delicate and soft at force. Becoming needful and angry as I lie in bed wishing it would all just stop for once. But I know it won' t stop, not until I make a visit to the woods. Tossing off the covers I slip into my jeans I had careless thrown on the floor before climbing into bed. Stuff into the corner was my shirt and sweatshirt from earlier in the day. I slip on my sneakers and open the door to the crisp October air. The whispers have become less angry, yet more insistent as I start walking toward the woods. My heart is hammering and cold sweat runs down my back. It is so silent, it gives me a familiar feeling of claustrophobia. It feels as though I am walking into a deep, dark cave, though the occasional glimpse of the moon proves that feeling wrong. The whispers are louder the deeper I trek in the forest, calling to me, urging me on. My mouth has gone dry, but I know there is no turning back now. To do so would only be worse than what I am enduring now. Ahead lies a clearing, with a simple pile of stones. The whispers have increased in frequency now, taunting me, describing my worst fears, my mistakes, my failures. All of these things are related, and located under this pile of stones. And every year these stones called to me. I stopped at the foot of the stones. Nothing in the clearing has changed from this time last year. I start to remove some of the rocks from the pile. Going deeper and deeper until a human skull is uncovered in the light of the moon. The whispers stopped and I hear a clear voice inside my head, as the jaw of the skull began to move. "Hello brother. "

Amidst a world enveloped in turmoil, a lone figure stood tall, gripping a shimmering blade with a fierce determination in their eyes. The weight of their oath hung heavy in the air as they vowed to change fate itself and forge a brighter future. The sacrifices of fallen heroes fueled their unwavering resolve to rewrite destiny's course.Bound by honor and a deep sense of purpose, they embarked on a perilous journey, each step marked by unwavering determination. With every swing of the blade, they fought against the relentless tide of darkness, their spirit unyielding. The echoes of the heroes' final battles reverberated within their heart, fueling an unquenchable fire that burned within.They sought to challenge the very fabric of fate, to unravel the threads that bound the world in despair. Their path was fraught with trials, adversaries, and seemingly insurmountable obstacles, yet their spirit remained unbroken. The weight of their vow carried them forward, an unbreakable tether to their mission.With each clash of steel, the echoes of past battles intertwined with their own struggle, a testament to the enduring legacy of those who had come before. They pressed on, undeterred, their unwavering resolve a beacon of hope in the face of overwhelming adversity.Through the tumultuous waves of destiny, they sailed, steadfast and unyielding. The journey was marked by triumphs and heart-wrenching setbacks, but their spirit remained indomitable. Each step brought them closer to their ultimate goal, the fulfillment of a vow to change fate and craft a better tomorrow.As they neared the culmination of their quest, they encountered an enigmatic figure, a guardian of the cosmic balance. A final, formidable challenge awaited, a test of their determination and strength of character. With the weight of their oath fueling every action, they faced their fate head-on, ready to rewrite the tapestry of existence.

Immediately upon walking in, it took 15 minutes for someone to ask if we were helped. When I mentioned that I wanted to learn more about a Zuma over in the corner, the dude at the desk was disinterested and unhelpful. nnWhen I tried to pay for the scooter and roll it out, an older woman stewed around and gave me annoyed looks for coming into the store and not calling ahead. When I tried to pay with a bank-issued check, I had to wait around for nearly 20 minutes before they told me that they have never seen a bank-issued check and sent me on my way. nnThe entire time I was there, it felt like they thought I just stole a bunch of money and were treating me like a crook. I am definitely not going back to complete the transaction. If I'm going to be buying an expensive piece of machinery, I really expect the service to at least be a little more enthusiastic. nnLike Nick T. said, I guess it's a good place if you wanna grab a used bike or know exactly what you want. If you're looking to broker a new bike, go to a bigger shop with salesmen that actually sound like they're living beings.

Wasps lock Joe Launchbury has been tipped as the ideal captain for England under new head coach Eddie Jones. According to former England scrum-half Matt Dawson, Launchbury has the "X factor" to lead the team to success. Wasps boss Dai Young has also backed the 24-year-old, saying he has the qualities to be a great captain. Launchbury has been capped 24 times for England and has impressed with his performances for Wasps. He is seen as a natural leader and has the respect of his peers. Former England winger Ugo Monye has also praised Launchbury, saying he is "battered emotionally and physically" after the World Cup but still wants to lead England. Jones is yet to decide on his captain, but Launchbury is a strong contender. Launchbury's leadership skills were evident during Wasps' victory over Saracens in the Premiership semi-final, where he led by example and inspired his team to victory. With his combination of talent, leadership skills, and work ethic, Launchbury could be the perfect choice to lead England under Jones.

" Hey everyone " says Fedora, the tubby, animated boy. His acne-pocked face and neckbeard blend his body seamlessly into a shirt which reads " I' m an atheist, debate me ". He waves to the camera, though this appears to have been too much physical exercise, and he sits down. " Today we' re going on a special adventure, we' re going to go to the store and pick up the new Richard Dawkins book. " He pauses, panting from the exhaustion of speaking for a prolonged period of time, and shoves a fingerless gloved hand into a bag of Doritos, the only sustenance he knows. " But how do we get there?! I know, we' ll ask /b/! Let' s look in my fedora and see if we can find /b/! " The boy pulls his hat off and, much like the scenes involving the backpack in the show' s predecessor, a series of small, dancing products appear. We see a bag of doritos, a small doggy-bag of weed, a signed copy of " The God Delusion ", a web browser window showing 4chan' s /b/ board and a small, framed locket in which rests a drawing of Hanako, from the popular visual novel Katawa Shoujo. " We need to wake up /b/, guys! can you yell' mods'? " *a pause, allowing for the audience to yell' mods' * " That' s great, look, we' ve got him up! " CUT TO web browser, we see Fedora the Explorer posing his question. " How do I get to the book store, /b/? " Amidst a torrent of " tits or GTFO " comments, we see three highlighted. *out of the basement *into mom' s car *into the bookstore Fedora looks to the camera and smiles, wiping his crumby hands over his duster coat and jean shorts. " That' s easy, out of the basement - " He pauses. " On second thoughts, let' s just get Amazon to deliver. " The camera pans out as we see fedora sit back in his chair and begin to shitpost violently.

Very recently in arXiv:0705.0303 Alday and Maldacena gave a string theoryprescription for computing (all) planar amplitudes in N=4 supersymmetric gaugetheory at strong coupling using the AdS/CFT correspondence. These amplitudesare determined by a classical string solution and contain a universalexponential factor involving the action of the classical string. On the gaugetheory side, expressions for perturbative amplitudes at strong coupling werepreviously proposed only for specific helicities of external particles -- themaximally helicity violating or MHV amplitudes. These follow from theexponential ansatz of Bern, Dixon and Smirnov for MHV amplitudes in N=4 SYM. Inthis paper we examine the amplitudes dependence on helicities andparticle-types of external states. We consider the prefactor of stringamplitudes and give arguments suggesting that the prefactor at strong couplingshould be the same as the Yang-Mills tree-level amplitude for the same process.This implies that scattering amplitudes in N=4 SYM simplify dramatically in thestrong coupling limit. It follows from our proposal that in this limit all (MHVand non-MHV) n-point amplitudes are given by the (known) tree-level Yang-Millsresult times the helicity-independent (and particle-type-independent) universalexponential.

Abstract:In this study, we investigate Quantum Electrodynamics (QED) for fields that obey a square root operator equation. We explore the implications of this unique mathematical formulation on the behavior of electromagnetic fields within the framework of QED. By analyzing the characteristics of such fields and their interaction with charged particles, we aim to gain a deeper understanding of the underlying physics. Our analysis reveals intriguing phenomena that emerge from this square root operator equation, including novel field configurations, modified scattering amplitudes, and altered energy spectra. Additionally, we examine the connections between this equation and other mathematical frameworks in theoretical physics, shedding light on potential applications beyond QED. Through this investigation, we contribute to the ongoing discourse on the fundamental properties of electromagnetic fields and open new avenues for future exploration in the realm of quantum field theory.

We present a new numerical code, ECHO, based on an Eulerian Conservative HighOrder scheme for time dependent three-dimensional general relativisticmagnetohydrodynamics (GRMHD) and magnetodynamics (GRMD). ECHO is aimed atproviding a shock-capturing conservative method able to work at an arbitrarylevel of formal accuracy (for smooth flows), where the other existing GRMHD andGRMD schemes yield an overall second order at most. Moreover, our goal is topresent a general framework, based on the 3+1 Eulerian formalism, allowing fordifferent sets of equations, different algorithms, and working in a genericspace-time metric, so that ECHO may be easily coupled to any solver forEinstein's equations. Various high order reconstruction methods are implementedand a two-wave approximate Riemann solver is used. The induction equation istreated by adopting the Upwind Constrained Transport (UCT) procedures,appropriate to preserve the divergence-free condition of the magnetic field inshock-capturing methods. The limiting case of magnetodynamics (also known asforce-free degenerate electrodynamics) is implemented by simply replacing thefluid velocity with the electromagnetic drift velocity and by neglecting thematter contribution to the stress tensor. ECHO is particularly accurate,efficient, versatile, and robust. It has been tested against severalastrophysical applications, including a novel test on the propagation of largeamplitude circularly polarized Alfven waves. In particular, we show thatreconstruction based on a Monotonicity Preserving filter applied to a fixed5-point stencil gives highly accurate results for smooth solutions, both inflat and curved metric (up to the nominal fifth order), while at the same timeproviding sharp profiles in tests involving discontinuities.

We present the results of the analysis of an archival 15 ks XMM-Newtonobservation of the nearby (z=0.057) radio-loud source 3C445, opticallyclassified as a Broad-Line Radio Galaxy. While the RGS data are of insufficientquality to allow a meaningful analysis, the EPIC data show a remarkable X-rayspectrum. The 2-10 keV continuum is described by a heavily absorbed (Nh~ 1e22 -1e23 cm-2) power law with photon index Gamma ~1.4, and strong (R~2) coldreflection. A narrow, unresolved Fe Kalpha emission line is detected,confirming previous findings, with EW 120eV. A soft excess is present below 2keV over the extrapolation of the hard X-ray power law, which we model with apower law with the same photon index as the hard power law, absorbed by acolumn density Nh=6e20 cm-2 in excess to Galactic. A host of emission lines arepresent below 2 keV, confirming previous indications from ASCA, due to H- andHe-like O, Mg, and Si. We attribute the origin of the lines to a photoionizedgas, with properties very similar to radio-quiet obscured AGN. Two differentionized media, or a single stratified medium, are required to fit the softX-ray data satisfactorily. The similarity of the X-ray spectrum of 3C445 toSeyferts underscores that the central engines of radio-loud and radio-quiet AGNsimilarly host both cold and warm gas.

An Aberdeen man who lost his life in an RAF drone strike targeting the so-called Islamic State group is being remembered by a childhood friend as someone who possessed an unwavering bravery and a complete lack of fear when it came to his own mortality. The devastating news of his death has left his family and friends in a state of shock and grief, as they try to come to terms with the loss of a loved one who had dedicated himself to the fight against terrorism.According to his childhood friend, who wishes to remain anonymous, the man displayed a remarkable courage that seemed to defy all comprehension. He said, "He had absolutely no fear of death. Even as children, he never hesitated to take risks or stand up for what he believed in."Their friendship began in their early school years, where they both grew up in Aberdeen and shared countless memories of their youth. They would spend hours discussing their dreams and aspirations, with his friend remembering how he always spoke passionately about wanting to make a real difference in the world."He believed wholeheartedly in the fight against terrorism," his childhood friend added. "He felt it was his duty to protect innocent lives from extremist ideologies, even if it meant sacrificing his own. It was a cause he held close to his heart."As news of his friend's death spread throughout the community, tributes poured in for the brave and selfless individual who had made the ultimate sacrifice. The heartfelt condolences from friends, family, and even strangers reflect the profound impact he had on those who knew him.The local community in Aberdeen plans to hold a memorial service to honor his memory and pay tribute to his commitment to the fight against terrorism. It will serve as a gathering for people to come together and share stories of his remarkable courage, celebrating a life that touched many hearts and left an indelible mark on the community.

I walked into the coffee shop, excited to get my morning caffeine fix. I had been here a few times before and had always enjoyed the friendly service and delicious coffee. As I approached the counter, I noticed a young woman, probably in her early 20s, standing behind the counter. She looked up at me and gave me a big smile, which I returned. I ordered my usual latte and waited patiently for her to make it.As she was finishing up my drink, I noticed that she was acting a bit strange. She seemed nervous and fidgety, glancing around the shop and avoiding eye contact. I thought maybe she was just having a bad day or was feeling a bit overwhelmed. However, when she handed me my latte, she also handed me a napkin with a note written on it.The note said, "I think you're cute. Would you like to go out sometime?" My initial reaction was shock and confusion. I didn't know how to respond, so I just smiled and said, "Um, thank you?" I took my latte and napkin and walked away, feeling a bit uncomfortable.I sat down at a table and started to enjoy my coffee, but I couldn't shake off the strange interaction. I couldn't believe that a barista would hit on me in such a forward way. It was unprofessional and made me feel uncomfortable. I didn't know if I should be flattered or annoyed.As I was sitting there, I noticed the barista glancing over at me a few times, smiling and giggling to herself. It was clear that she was trying to get my attention, but I didn't know how to react. I didn't want to encourage her, but I also didn't want to be rude.I finished my coffee and decided to leave. As I was walking out, the barista called out to me, "Hey, do you want my number? I'd love to grab coffee with you sometime!" I was taken aback by her forwardness and didn't know what to say. I smiled and said, "Um, no thanks. I'm flattered, but I'm not interested."She looked disappointed, but I didn't care. I had never experienced anything like this before and didn't know how to handle it.

The seafront line opened in 1865 to provide a link from the railway station to the quay and Channel Island ferries.The last regular service ran in 1987 but the line was used for special occasions until 1999.Andy Spreadbury believes re-launching the line would also help with parking problems in the town during the summer.The tracks to the quayside remain in place, making the route still viable, said Mr Spreadbury, who worked on it in the late 1970s.Network Rail said while the main line to Weymouth is electrified, the seaside track is not, which could make a reopening of it problematic.He added: "It's unique, it's the last one of its kind in the country, at this gauge anyway."It can be a major tourist attraction because it's so unique."We're not yet looking at costs. We are just putting it out to consultation to see what people in Weymouth think."There have been previous attempts to reopen the line but Weymouth and Portland Council said none had been financially viable.It had voted to acquire the track from Network Rail in a Â£50,000 deal in 2009 but the sale never went through, a spokesman said.A Network Rail spokesman said: "If the council decided they wanted to do something with it we are happy to do that, whether its keeping it as a railway, cycle track or tramway."But if the local authority, community or an operator said they wanted to see trains back, we would work with them to achieve that but it would depend on a solid business case."The most recent attempt to reopen the line was made by enthusiasts who wanted it used to bring spectators to the sailing events during the 2012 Olympic Games.

Dynamic phase transitions (DPTs) have recently gained significant attention in the study of complex systems. In this article, we investigate the role of the conjugate field and the fluctuation-dissipation relation in the context of the dynamic phase transition occurring in the two-dimensional kinetic Ising model. By subjecting the model to an oscillating external field, we observe a transition from a symmetric to an asymmetric dynamic phase with the emergence of an oscillatory behavior in the system's order parameter. Through extensive Monte Carlo simulations, we analyze the dynamics of the system and examine the effects of the conjugate field on the critical behavior of the DPT. Our findings reveal that the critical properties of the phase transition are influenced by the choice of the conjugate field, yielding distinct universality classes for different conjugate field configurations.Furthermore, we investigate the fluctuation-dissipation relation in the presence of the dynamic phase transition. By examining the time-dependent magnetization autocorrelation function, we extract the scaling properties of the fluctuation-dissipation relation and explore its connection to the critical exponents characterizing the DPT. Interestingly, our results indicate that the fluctuation-dissipation relation maintains its validity in the asymmetric dynamic phase, albeit with modified scaling behavior. We provide a comprehensive analysis of the numerical results, supported by finite-size scaling analysis and critical exponent estimates.Overall, our study demonstrates the intricate interplay between the conjugate field and the fluctuation-dissipation relation in the two-dimensional kinetic Ising model with a dynamic phase transition. The observed distinct universality classes and the modified scaling behavior emphasize the rich phenomenology associated with DPTs and provide valuable insights into the understanding of nonequilibrium phase transitions in complex systems.

Sarah sat down at her desk, a pen in hand and a blank sheet of paper before her. The writing prompt instructed her to name the top ten things on her bucket list. She smiled, excited to pour her dreams onto the page. Taking a deep breath, Sarah began to write. 1. Travel the world and visit every continent, immersing myself in different cultures and experiencing new adventures.2. Skydive from a plane and feel the exhilarating rush of freefall before safely landing on the ground.3. Learn to play a musical instrument and serenade others with beautiful melodies.4. Write and publish a book, sharing my stories and imagination with the world.5. Volunteer in a meaningful way, making a positive impact on the lives of others.6. Watch the Northern Lights paint the night sky with their breathtaking colors.7. Swim with dolphins and marvel at their grace and intelligence.8. Learn a new language fluently, opening doors to connect with people from diverse backgrounds.9. Start a family and create a loving and nurturing home for my children.10. Find inner peace and live a life filled with joy, gratitude, and fulfillment.As Sarah finished writing, she read over her list with a sense of excitement and determination. These were her aspirations, her goals to strive for. Each item represented a desire burning within her, waiting to be realized. She folded the paper neatly and placed it in her bucket, knowing that with dedication and perseverance, she could turn these dreams into beautiful memories.

Elsa, the queen of Arendelle, sat on her throne, staring at the map of the world. She had always dreamed of expanding her kingdom and becoming an empress, and now she finally had the chance. With her sister Anna by her side and the support of her people, she was ready to make her move.She called for her advisor, Kristoff, and asked him to gather the council. When they were all assembled, she announced her plan to expand Arendelle' s borders and become an empire. The council was divided on the issue, with some members supporting Elsa' s plan and others opposing it.Elsa listened to their arguments, but she was determined to see her plan through. She reminded them of the benefits that an empire would bring to Arendelle, such as increased trade and prosperity. She also assured them that she would rule with fairness and justice.In the end, the council voted in favor of Elsa' s plan. Elsa was overjoyed and immediately began to make preparations for her conquest. She gathered her army and set out to conquer the neighboring kingdoms.One by one, the kingdoms fell to Elsa' s army. She was a skilled and ruthless warrior, and her enemies were no match for her. Soon, she had conquered a vast empire, stretching from the North Pole to the South Pole.Elsa was now Empress Elsa, and she ruled her empire with an iron fist. She was a powerful and feared ruler, and her enemies trembled at the sound of her name. But even though she had achieved her dream, she was not happy. She missed her old life in Arendelle, and she longed for the days when she was just a simple queen.One day, Elsa decided to visit Arendelle. When she arrived, she was shocked to see how much it had changed. The city was now a bustling metropolis, and the people were living in luxury. Elsa was amazed at how much her sister had accomplished in her absence.Elsa met with Anna and they talked for hours. Anna told her about all the changes that had happened in Arendelle, and Elsa told her about her conquests.

Two-dimensional correlation spectroscopy (2DCS) based on the nonlinearoptical response of excitons to sequences of ultrafast pulses, has thepotential to provide some unique insights into carrier dynamics insemiconductors. The most prominent feature of 2DCS, cross peaks, can best beunderstood using a sum-over-states picture involving the many-body eigenstates.However, the optical response of semiconductors is usually calculated bysolving truncated equations of motion for dynamical variables, which result ina quasiparticle picture. In this work we derive Green's function expressionsfor the four wave mixing signals generated in various phase-matching directionsand use them to establish the connection between the two pictures. The formalconnection with Frenkel excitons (hard-core bosons) and vibrational excitons(soft-core bosons) is pointed out.

A chaotic scene unfolded on a bustling high street in north-east London when a car careened into a charity building, leading to the arrest of a man. The incident occurred on Monday afternoon, leaving witnesses and onlookers in shock. Eyewitnesses reported seeing a grey Volkswagen Golf barreling down the street before crashing into the building, which housed a local charity. The impact caused significant damage to the structure, raising concerns about the safety of those inside. Emergency services swiftly arrived at the scene, attending to a pregnant woman who was seriously injured as a result of the collision. Fortunately, initial assessments suggest that her unborn child was unharmed.Essex Police wasted no time in apprehending a 25-year-old man in connection with the incident. He was arrested on suspicion of dangerous driving, assault, fraud by false representation, and failing to stop at the scene of an accident. Authorities confirmed that the man was being held in custody at a local police station and was being questioned about the events leading up to the crash. Investigations are currently underway to determine the motive behind the alleged fraud attempt and the circumstances surrounding the collision.Detective Inspector Stuart Truss of the Essex Police urged any potential witnesses or anyone with information to come forward. He emphasized the importance of gathering as much evidence as possible to build a strong case. "The shop was very busy at that time of day, and there were also a lot of people in the area who may have seen what happened," he said. The incident serves as a stark reminder of the potential dangers lurking in everyday situations and highlights the need for vigilance and safety precautions in public spaces. The affected charity and the local community are now left to pick up the pieces and recover from the unexpected mayhem that unfolded on their doorstep.

A farmer in Western Australia had an unexpected encounter when a wedge-tailed eagle targeted his drone, causing it to plummet to the ground. The incident occurred during a routine drone flight over the farmer's property, intended to monitor the land and crops. As the drone soared through the sky, it caught the attention of the majestic eagle, which swiftly launched an aerial assault. With its immense wingspan and sharp talons, the eagle swooped down and struck the drone, causing it to lose control and crash. The farmer, astonished by the eagle's display of aggression, managed to retrieve the damaged drone and capture the aftermath on video. Wedge-tailed eagles are known for their territorial behavior, often defending their airspace against perceived threats, which in this case included the unfamiliar intrusion of a drone. While unfortunate for the farmer, the incident serves as a reminder of the power and agility of these magnificent birds of prey.

Once upon a time in the realm of politics, there were four extraordinary RPG parties vying for power. The Righteous Reformers, the Noble Guardians, the Sly Strategists, and the Enigmatic Progressives. The Righteous Reformers were the valiant knights, crusading for justice and equality, their leaders clad in shining armor, wielding their swords of righteousness. The Noble Guardians were the stalwart defenders, devoted to upholding tradition and protecting the realm from external threats. Their leaders were wise and noble, adorned in regal attire, wielding shields of loyalty. The Sly Strategists were the cunning rogues, masters of manipulation and calculated moves. Their leaders wore disguises, hiding in the shadows, armed with daggers of deceit. The Enigmatic Progressives were the mystical sorcerers, harnessing the power of change and innovation. Their leaders donned mysterious robes, wielding staffs of enlightenment. As the political quests unfolded, these RPG parties clashed, each bringing their unique abilities and agendas, shaping the fate of the realm. People marveled at the spectacle, witnessing the battles of wit, charisma, and ideology. The realm's future hung in the balance, as the RPG parties fought for dominance, employing their own strategies and alliances, with victories and setbacks dictating the course of history. And so, in this grand political RPG adventure, the destiny of the realm lay in the hands of these extraordinary parties, their stories etched in the annals of politics, forever remembered.

Determinations of beryllium abundance in stars, together with lithium,provide a key tool to investigate the so far poorly understood extra-mixingprocesses at work in stellar interiors. We measured Be in three openclusters,complementing existing Be surveys, and aiming at gathering a morecomplete empirical scenario of the evolution of Be as a function of stellar ageand temperature. Specifically, we analyzed VLT/UVES spectra of members of NGC2516, the Hyades, and M 67 to determine their Be and Li abundances. In thefirst two clusters we focused on stars cooler than 5400 K, while the M 67sample includes stars warmer than 6150 K, as well as two subgiants and two bluestragglers. We also computed the evolution of Be for a 0.9 Mo star based onstandard evolutionary models. We find different emprical behaviours for starsin different temperature bins and ages. Stars warmer than 6150 K show Bedepletion and follow a Be vs. Li correlation while Be is undepleted in stars inthe ~6150-5600 K range. NGC 2516 members cooler than 5400 K have not depletedany Be, but older Hyades of similar temperature do show some depletion. Be isseverely depleted in the subgiants and blue stragglers. The results for warmstars are in agreement with previous studies, supporting the hypothesis thatmixing in this temperature regime is driven by rotation. The same holds for thetwo subgiants that have evolved from the "Li gap". This mechanism is insteadnot the dominant one for solar-type stars. We show that Be depletion of coolHyades cannot simply be explained by the effect of increasing depth of theconvective zone. Finally, the different Be content of the two blue stragglerssuggests that they have formed by two different processes (i.e., collisions vs.binary merging).

In this article, we delve into the concept of $p$-adic multiresolution analysis (MRA) and its implications. We begin by introducing a refinement equation that gives rise to a refinable function, namely the characteristic function of the unit disc. This equation captures the interesting property that the characteristic function of the unit disc can be expressed as a sum of $p$ characteristic functions of disjoint discs with a radius of $p^{-1}$. This refinement equation serves as the foundation for generating a $p$-adic MRA.We specifically focus on the case when $p=2$ and thoroughly examine the 2-adic Haar MRA. Unlike its real Haar MRA counterpart, our $p$-adic Haar MRA possesses a refinable function that is 1-periodic, a departure from the real setting. Consequently, we discover the existence of infinitely many distinct 2-adic orthonormal wavelet bases in ${\cL}^2(\bQ_2)$ that are generated by the same Haar MRA, and we provide detailed descriptions of all these bases.Expanding our analysis, we extend the construction of orthonormal bases to multidimensional settings in ${\cL}^2(\bQ_2^n)$ using the tensor product of one-dimensional MRAs. Moreover, we derive a criterion for determining if a multidimensional $p$-adic wavelet can function as an eigenfunction for a pseudo-differential operator. Additionally, we establish that these wavelets can also serve as eigenfunctions of the Taibleson multidimensional fractional operator, further enhancing their potential applications.The findings presented in this article lay the groundwork for the extensive utilization of our $p$-adic wavelet bases in various practical applications. Their significance is evident in domains such as $p$-adic models that involve pseudo-differential operators and fractional operators. By leveraging the properties and characteristics of these bases, we can enhance understanding and employ them effectively across multiple contexts, leading to advancements in research and practical implementations.

I sat alone in my cell, the cold, grey walls closing in on me. The sound of clanging doors and distant shouts echoed through the prison, a constant reminder of my confinement. I had given up hope, resigned to the fact that I would spend the rest of my days in this bleak, unforgiving place.But then, one day, something unexpected happened. As I sat down to eat my breakfast, I noticed a small piece of paper nestled among the eggs and toast. My heart raced as I picked it up, my fingers trembling with anticipation.The note was brief, but its message was clear: one of the guards knew I was innocent, and he was going to try to help me escape. My mind raced with questions: who was this guard? How could I trust him? And most importantly, how were we going to pull off an escape?Despite my doubts, I couldn't shake the feeling of hope that had been reignited within me. I had to take a chance, had to believe that this guard was my only chance at freedom.So, I waited, my heart pounding in my chest, as the days ticked by at a glacial pace. I barely slept, my mind consumed by thoughts of the escape.Finally, the day arrived. The guard, a tall, imposing figure with a gruff demeanor, appeared outside my cell. "It's time," he said, his voice low and urgent.I followed him, my heart racing, as he led me through a maze of corridors and stairwells. We passed other guards, who eyed us suspiciously, but my guard seemed to know exactly what he was doing.We reached a small door that I had never noticed before. The guard produced a key, and with a quick twist, the door swung open. We were outside, the cool night air a welcome relief after the stifling heat of the prison.We moved quickly, our footsteps echoing through the deserted streets. I couldn't believe it: we were really doing it. We were escaping.But, as we turned a corner, a loud shout echoed through the night. My heart sank as I saw a group of guards running towards us, their guns drawn.

Mr Hemingway, his wife Gerardine, and the HemingwayDesign team will create the overall scheme and branding for the disused Dreamland site in Margate.It is hoped that stage one of the project to turn the site into a Â£10m heritage park will open in 2014.Mr Hemingway said he was "excited about bringing a new vibrant life to an iconic British seaside institution"."Having visited Margate over the past few months and seen the grassroots creative revolution that is taking place, we are thrilled, nay cock a hoop, at the potential of the place and can't wait to get stuck in," he said."We fully understand the scale of the task but with the creative community of Margate and environs we can deliver something of international significance."The park will be run by the Dreamland Trust, which will be given the site by Thanet District Council for a peppercorn rent.Chairman Nick Laister said he had no doubt HemingwayDesign would bring the trust's proposals for the site to life, exceeding its expectations."Wayne and Gerardine and their team have a genuine passion for the British seaside and for the Dreamland Margate project," he said."I am delighted to welcome them on to our team."The heritage amusement park is seen as vital in the regeneration of Margate, along with the Turner Contemporary art gallery which opened further along the seafront last year.

**Assistant****Amy Winehouse Documentary Honored by European Film Academy**A controversial documentary about the life and death of British singer Amy Winehouse has been honored by the European Film Academy. The film, which premiered at the Cannes Film Festival in May, has been praised by critics for its unflinching look at Winehouse's struggles with addiction and mental health issues. However, it has also been criticized by some for its portrayal of Winehouse's family. The European Film Academy has defended the film, saying that it is an important piece of cinema that deserves to be seen.

Mr McGuigan was shot dead last month and police said they believed members of the Provisional IRA were involved.Bobby Storey, Eddie Copeland, Brian Gillen had been arrested in Belfast on Wednesday. The unnamed woman, who is 50, was detained on Thursday.Mr Storey, a former IRA prisoner, is the northern chairman of Sinn Féin.In a tweet, Mr Storey's lawyer John Finucane said: "After two days in Antrim, my client Bobby Storey has been freed. No evidence was put at any stage and my client will be suing for unlawful arrest."The Police Service of Northern Ireland's Chief Constable George Hamilton said last month that detectives believe Provisional IRA members had a role in Mr McGuigan Sr's murder.But he added there was no evidence at that stage that the killing had been sanctioned at a senior level of the organisation.Sinn Féin said the IRA no longer exists after it ordered an end to its armed campaign in 2005.A total of 17 people have been arrested in connection with Mr McGuigan Sr's murder.

I've eaten here and stopped in for drinks a couple times over the years and I just haven't been super impressed by the place. nnThe food has been decent - a few years back I splurged on an enormous fried chicken sandwich and definitely enjoyed it, glutton that I am - but their meals and appetizers alike have admittedly never "wowed" me. And while I enjoy their cocktails and would consider stopping in just for drinks more often, the problem is that things are way overpriced here. Once I stopped in with friends after a movie and one drink cost me $10 here. Not even a large one, either! Sorry, but I find that to be kind of ridiculous, especially since there's cheaper places to get drinks in the area. nnStill, the food and drinks are definitely a-ok albeit overpriced, so this place would be rated 3 stars in my mind IF I could hear myself think when I eat here. Not cool. nnSo while the food and drinks are fine enough the loud venue and prices mean that this is definitely not one of my favorite places - and given the other options we have at the Waterfront I don't expect to be back in any time soon.

We study two recently proposed equations of state (EOS) which are obtainedfrom high temperature QCD, and show how they can be adapted to use them formaking predictions for relativistic heavy ion collisions. The method involvesextracting equilibrium distribution functions for quarks and gluons from theEOS, which in turn will allow a determination of the transport and other bulkproperties of the quark gluon plasma. Simultaneously, the method also yields aquasi particle description of interacting quarks and gluons. The first EOS isperturbative in the QCD coupling constant and has contributions of $O(g^5)$.The second EOS is an improvement over the first, with contributions upto $O(g^6 ln(\frac{1}{g}))$; it incorporates the nonperturbative hard thermalcontributions. The interaction effects are shown to be captured entirely by theeffective chemical potentials for the gluons and the quarks, in both the cases.The chemical potential is seen to be highly sensitive to the EOS. As anapplication, we determine the screening lengths which are, indeed the mostimportant diagnostics for QGP. The screening lengths are seen to behavedrastically differently depending on the EOS considered., and yield, therefore,a way to distinguish the two equations of state in heavy ion collisions.

The incident happened at about 17:20 on the eastbound carriageway of the city's West Approach Road.A female passenger who also fell from the motorbike was uninjured.The carriageway remains closed while police officers carry out crash investigation work. Police Scotland urged anyone who saw the incident to come forward.In particular, officers want to trace a male cyclist who was seen in the area, having joined the West Approach Road at its junction with Roseburn Street.Insp Brenda Sinclair, of Police Scotland, said: "Tragically, a man has passed away as a result of this incident and my thoughts are with his family at this sad time."Our inquiries into this matter are ongoing and I ask anyone with relevant information to contact police on 101."I also appeal directly to the male cyclist to contact police immediately."

Celtic star Kieran Tierney has committed his future to the club, signing a new five-year contract that will keep him at Parkhead until 2023. The 21-year-old defender, who has been with Celtic since he was a young boy, said he never considered leaving the club despite interest from elsewhere.Tierney has established himself as one of the top young defenders in the game, and his performances for Celtic have earned him rave reviews. He has made over 100 appearances for the Hoops, helping the club win three consecutive Scottish Premiership titles.The full-back, who can also play in midfield, said he was delighted to sign a new deal with Celtic, calling it "a dream come true." "I've been at this club since I was a wee boy, and I can't imagine playing for anyone else," he said. "I'm just happy to get it all sorted and focus on playing football now."Tierney's commitment to Celtic will come as a relief to fans, who were worried that he might leave the club following interest from English Premier League sides. The defender, who has also been capped by Scotland, said he never considered leaving, however."I've always said that I want to play for Celtic, and that's still the case," he said. "I love everything about this club – the fans, the atmosphere, the history. It's an honor to play for Celtic, and I'm just happy to be here."Tierney's new contract is a significant investment in the club's future, with the defender seen as a key player for years to come. Celtic manager Neil Lennon hailed Tierney's commitment, calling him "one of the best young players in the country."Lennon said, "We're delighted that Kieran has committed his future to the club. He's a fantastic player, and he's going to be a huge asset for us moving forward."Tierney's new deal comes as Celtic prepare to face off against Lazio in the Europa League. The Hoops face a tough task, but with Tierney on board, they'll be confident of their chances.

Coffee. That's what I need right now. I stumbled into the kitchen, fumbling to get the coffee maker started. The sun was going down, and the sky was colored in red as blood. Yesterday was a blur, and today was shaping up to be just as chaotic. I poured myself a cup of the steaming liquid and took a sip, hoping it would help clear my head. Nope, not working. I glanced around the messy apartment, trying to piece together the events of last night. What did I do last night? The room smelled like smoke, and there were empty beer cans scattered on the floor. That can't be good. I checked my phone. Missed calls and unread messages bombarded the screen. Damn, I must have left a really bad impression. I sat down on the couch, clutching my coffee as if it held the answers to the questions swirling in my mind. Memories started to trickle back. A heated argument with a friend, voices raised and accusations hurled. That knot in my stomach tightened. What did I say? What did I do? Anxiety gnawed at me. I decided to take a walk, hoping fresh air would clear my head and soothe my jumbled thoughts. As I stepped outside, the cool breeze hit my face, providing some relief. I walked aimlessly, lost in a haze of self-doubt. Who exactly did I piss off yesterday? The streets were bustling with people, but their faces blurred together, unrecognizable. Suddenly, a distant sound caught my attention. Sirens. Growing louder by the second. Panic spread through the crowd, a wave of uncertainty. Everyone stopped in their tracks, trying to make sense of the chaos unfolding before them. Smoke billowed in the distance, darkening the sky further. We did everything we could to contain the fire, but it raged on relentlessly. Buildings crumbled, turning into piles of ash and rubble. The flames danced and licked at everything in their path, devouring memories and dreams. Fear consumed the streets, as people ran frantically, seeking safety.

One night I was watching the Travel channel, they had a show called Top 10 Places to Pig Out. As someone who loves indulging in mouthwatering food, I couldn't resist tuning in to see which food haven they were showcasing. Little did I know, my cravings were about to hit the jackpot when they featured a restaurant called "The Food Factory."The moment they showcased this culinary gem, I knew I had to visit it and experience their gastronomical delights firsthand. So, the following weekend, I rallied up a group of food-loving friends, and we embarked on our journey to "The Food Factory."As we arrived at the restaurant, the first thing that caught our attention was the lively ambiance. The place was buzzing with people, laughter filling the air, and the tantalizing aroma of food wafting through the room. It was a welcoming sight, and our appetites soared with anticipation.The menu boasted an extensive range of dishes, catering to every palate. From juicy burgers to cheesy pizzas, spicy curries to sizzling steaks, they had it all. Deciding what to order was like being a kid in a candy store, overwhelmed with countless delicious options.We started off with some appetizers to tickle our taste buds. The nachos arrived at our table, loaded with gooey cheese, tangy salsa, and a dollop of cool sour cream. The first bite was like a flavor explosion in my mouth, the crispy tortilla chips adding the perfect crunch to the harmony of flavors.Moving on, we ordered their signature dish, the "Ultimate Meat Platter." When it arrived, we couldn't help but gasp in awe at the mountain of succulent ribs, tender chicken wings, and juicy sausages laid before us. It was a meat lover's dream come true. UFC champion Conor McGregor has taken a significant step towards a potential showdown with legendary boxer Floyd Mayweather after being granted a boxing licence by the California State Athletic Commission. This development has fueled speculation about a potential clash between the two combat sports superstars. McGregor, 28, has previously expressed his desire to fight Mayweather, while the undefeated former boxing champion has also showcased interest in the mega-fight.The acquisition of the boxing licence now opens the door for McGregor to compete professionally in the sport of boxing within the United States. However, the Irishman remains under contract with the UFC, which means any fight against Mayweather would require the approval and cooperation of the mixed martial arts organization. This could potentially involve negotiations for a co-promotion or the blessing of the UFC.The proposed McGregor-Mayweather showdown has garnered immense attention and anticipation from both the boxing and MMA communities. Mayweather retired from the sport in 2015 with an unblemished 49-0 record, while McGregor achieved the remarkable feat of becoming the first dual-division champion in the history of the UFC in November. The clash between these two charismatic and influential figures would undoubtedly be a lucrative and headline-grabbing event.Speculation surrounding the potential fight intensified after UFC president Dana White announced that he would be willing to pay both fighters $25 million each to participate in the event. However, conflicting reports emerged, with Mayweather denying any agreement had been reached and challenging McGregor to "get the fight done." Mayweather suggested that McGregor should first take care of business with the UFC and have his representatives contact his team.While the financial details of the proposed fight are yet to be finalized, both McGregor and Mayweather have expressed their eagerness to make the contest happen.

Police in Andratx had been searching for Noakes, who has Alzheimer's disease, after they were alerted by the 81-year-old's wife Vicky at about 9am local time.Local police told the BBC he was spotted by a helicopter at around 18:45 local time close to his home.He is now in hospital and his wife is with him.An update on the town's Facebook page said that Noakes had been taken to hospital and was in a stable condition.Noakes presented the BBC children's show for 12 years from 1966 to 1978.Police and civil guard had been searching for him in the village. They said his wife was worried "because of his state of health and the heat, which is around 35C (95F) today."Two photos of Noakes had been posted on the local council's Facebook page asking people to look out for him.Noakes moved to Majorca with his wife after a round the world yacht trip was cut short when their boat was badly damaged by a giant wave off the coast of North Africa.They had embarked on the trip after Noakes quit Blue Peter in 1978 and Go With Noakes 18 months later.Noakes, who trained as an aircraft engine fitter before training as an actor, was 31 when he made his first appearance on BBC One's Blue Peter.He became a firm favourite with fans for his friendship with his border collie Shep and became the show's longest-running presenter.Famous for his on-air efforts to calm down his lively dog, Noakes was often heard saying "Get down Shep!" which prompted the Barron Knights' 1978 song of the same name.

We examine the metastable liquid phase of a supercooled gold nanocluster bystudying the free energy landscape using the largest solid-like embryo as anorder parameter. Just below freezing, the free energy exhibits a local minimumat small embryo sizes and a maximum at larger embryo sizes which denotes thecritical embryo size. At T=660K the free energy becomes a monotonicallydecreasing function of the order parameter as the liquid phase becomesunstable, indicating we have reached a spinodal. In contrast to the usualmean-field theory predictions, the size of the critical embryo remains finiteas the spinodal is approached. We also calculate the rate of nucleation,independently from our free energy calculations, and observe a rapid increasein its temperature dependence when the free energy barrier is in the order of$kT$. This supports the idea that freezing becomes a barrierless process aroundthe spinodal temperature.

We carry out the non-perturbative renormalization of the chromo-magneticoperator in Heavy Quark Effective Theory. At order 1/m of the expansion, theoperator is responsible for the mass splitting between the pseudoscalar andvector B mesons. We obtain its two-loop anomalous dimension in a Schr"odingerfunctional scheme by successive one-loop conversions to the lattice MS schemeand the MS-bar scheme. We then compute the scale evolution of the operatornon-perturbatively in the N_f=0 theory between $\mu \approx 0.3$ GeV and $\mu\approx 100$ GeV, where contact is made with perturbation theory. The overallrenormalization factor that converts the bare lattice operator to itsrenormalization group invariant form is given for the Wilson gauge action andtwo standard discretizations of the heavy-quark action. As an application, wefind that this factor brings the previous quenched predictions of the B* - Bmass splitting closer to the experimental value than found with a perturbativerenormalization. The same renormalization factor is applicable to thespin-dependent potentials of Eichten and Feinberg.

The development at Thainstone, near Inverurie, could create hundreds of jobs.ANM Group said the new business park - with new office buildings and parking - has been in the planning for several years.Life at Thainstone was the focus of a six-part BBC Scotland behind-the-scenes documentary - The Mart - earlier this year.It featured those who work there and the farmers who rely on the auctioneers to help sell their livestock.ANM Group chairman Pat Machray said: "The proposed expansion of Thainstone Business Park has been in our plans for a number of years and is the next natural step for the site."In 2012, the local area plan allocated this land for development, and we are thrilled to see it finally beginning to take shape."We have been a major employer and contributor to the north east economy for more than 140 years, and as a farmer-owned co-operative, we continually strive to improve the business to provide the best service for our 5,000 members and 20,000 customers."If approved, work is due to commence later this year, with the first building due for completion in early 2018.

In this study, the fusion process is investigated using the preequilibrium giant dipole resonance within the framework of time-dependent Hartree-Fock (TDHF) theory. The TDHF approach provides a microscopic description of the nuclear dynamics, allowing for a detailed analysis of the fusion process. The preequilibrium giant dipole resonance, arising from the collective motion of nucleons during the formation of composite systems, is considered as a key ingredient in understanding fusion reactions.By employing the TDHF formalism, the time evolution of the nuclear system is simulated, taking into account the coupling between the intrinsic motion and the collective degrees of freedom. The properties of the preequilibrium giant dipole resonance, such as its energy distribution, strength function, and time evolution, are studied in various fusion reactions. The effects of different nuclear potentials and initial conditions on the fusion process are also investigated.The results show that the presence of the preequilibrium giant dipole resonance significantly influences the fusion dynamics. It is found that the excitation energy of the resonance, as well as its coupling with other degrees of freedom, affects the fusion cross sections and barrier heights. Furthermore, the interplay between the fusion and breakup channels is explored, shedding light on the competition between these processes.The TDHF calculations provide valuable insights into the role of the preequilibrium giant dipole resonance in heavy-ion fusion reactions. The findings contribute to a deeper understanding of the microscopic mechanisms underlying fusion processes and have implications for nuclear astrophysics, stellar nucleosynthesis, and the synthesis of superheavy elements. The theoretical framework developed in this work can serve as a basis for future investigations of fusion reactions involving exotic nuclei and nonstandard energy regimes.

**Abstract**This work examines the consequences of strong electronic correlations in molecular junctions, where the small number of channels makes them more susceptible to Coulomb blockade and Kondo screening effects. Using the nonequilibrium equation-of-motion technique, we show how the interplay of these correlation effects can drastically change the charge and spin transport properties of a single-molecule device. We focus on two different limits, a Coulomb blockade dominated regime at low temperatures, and a Kondo regime at higher temperatures. We find that in the Coulomb blockade regime, the transport properties of molecular junctions exhibit pronounced zero-bias anomalies, which are strongly suppressed in the Kondo regime. We show that these distinct features can be used to discriminate between different interaction regimes in molecular junctions. Our work demonstrates the importance of correlation effects in molecular junctions and provides a framework for their investigation.**Keywords:** Molecular electronics, Coulomb blockade, Kondo effect, Nonequilibrium equation-of-motion technique, Charge transport, Spin transport

In this paper, the study of the gravitational collapse of cylindricallydistributed two perfect fluid system has been carried out. It is assumed thatthe collapsing speeds of the two fluids are very large. We explore thiscondition by using the high-speed approximation scheme. There arise two cases,i.e., bounded and vanishing of the ratios of the pressures with densities oftwo fluids given by $c_s, d_s$. It is shown that the high-speed approximationscheme breaks down by non-zero pressures $p_1, p_2$ when $c_s, d_s$ are boundedbelow by some positive constants. The failure of the high-speed approximationscheme at some particular time of the gravitational collapse suggests theuncertainity on the evolution at and after this time. In the bounded case, thenaked singularity formation seems to be impossible for the cylindrical twoperfect fluids. For the vanishing case, if a linear equation of state is used,the high-speed collapse does not break down by the effects of the pressures andconsequently a naked singularity forms. This work provides the generalisationof the results already given by Nakao and Morisawa [1] for the perfect fluid.

Seriously, I walked into this Giant Eagle with high hopes, expecting to find a grocery store that would blow my mind. But let me tell you, I was sorely disappointed. From the moment I entered, it felt like I had stepped back in time to the 90s. The place was outdated, dusty, and lacked any semblance of modernity. The shelves were poorly stocked, and I couldn't find half the items on my shopping list. It was like a scavenger hunt gone wrong.To make matters worse, the staff seemed oblivious to the customers' needs. I had to wander around aimlessly, desperately searching for someone to assist me. When I finally managed to track down an employee, they were unhelpful and disinterested in providing any assistance. It was clear that customer service was not a priority for this particular Giant Eagle.But what bothered me the most was the inflated prices. I mean, seriously, who do they think they are? I found myself comparing prices to other grocery stores in the area, and Giant Eagle was consistently more expensive. I couldn't justify paying those exorbitant prices for subpar service and a lackluster shopping experience.In conclusion, this Giant Eagle left a lot to be desired. I wouldn't recommend it to anyone unless they enjoy wasting their time and money. This paper investigates the sensing capacity of sensor networks for linear observation models with fixed signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). We first introduce the concept of sensing capacity, which refers to the minimum number of sensors required to detect the state of an environment with a desired level of accuracy. We then derive a lower bound on the sensing capacity for linear observation models with fixed SNR, which is based on the insight that a fixed sensor configuration encodes all states of the environment. Our result reveals that the sensing capacity expression differs significantly from the channel capacity, due to the fact that codewords are dependent and non-identically distributed. We further discuss the implications of our result for sensor selection and demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework through simulations. Our work provides a new perspective on the fundamental limits of sensor networks and has important implications for the design and deployment of sensor networks in various applications.

FHM was launched in 1985 under the title For Him Magazine. Zoo was launched in 2004.An online statement from publisher Bauer Media read: "Unfortunately it's true and it has been announced today the intention to suspend publication of FHM."The statement added that it had been "an absolute joy producing the magazine over the years"."Thank you for all your support, we will keep you updated with developments over the coming weeks."During the 1990s, FHM dominated the men's market and was published in 27 countries, including Pakistan and India.It claimed that its annual poll of the 100 Sexiest Women in the World "helped propel the careers of many well-known actresses, musicians and models".The magazine industry has been particularly vulnerable to online competition, and "lads' magazines" have been struggling to hold on to their readers.In the last 10 years, FHM's circulation has fallen from about 500,000 to below 100,000. In the last 12 months, rivals Nuts and Loaded have both closed their doors.The publishers said: "Men's media habits have continually moved towards mobile and social." However, it did not specifically blame the online market for its closure.It said that FHM and Zoo had a combined digital audience of more than five million.

The New Zealander takes charge of Scotland for the final time on Saturday, when Italy visit Murrayfield at the end of the Six Nations campaign."He's certainly leaving Scotland in a better state than he found it," said Nicol of Cotter's three-year reign."He has brought reality and a belief with the improvements he's made. He was so close to a World Cup semi-final."Cotter will switch to French Top 14 side Montpellier in the summer, with Gregor Townsend moving to Murrayfield from his role as head coach at Glasgow Warriors."The team is playing better, the players have developed in his three years and he's done a great job, in my opinion," the former scrum-half told BBC Radio Scotland."Let's just ignore what happened at Twickenham last Saturday (the 61-21 defeat to England) because if Scotland win tomorrow this will have been a great Six Nations."Potentially, with results elsewhere, we could finish second, which would be a fantastic result."Gregor Townsend will be taking over with a really good squad, good age demographic. They are playing really good rugby and they can play even better. I think the future is very bright."Nicol believes the Scotland players will be motivated to rediscover the form they showed in the home wins against Ireland and Wales and the 22-16 defeat by France in Paris."They'll be desperate to go out and prove to themselves, to the coaches, to the Scottish public, that what we saw at Twickenham was not the real Scotland," he said, looking forward to the match against the Azzurri."I'll put the England game down to a very bad day at the office, but I expect us to get back to the levels we showed in the first three games, playing with attacking prowess, with a bit of bite in defence and tactically being spot on."I expect a different Scotland to what we saw last week."

A passenger train crashed into the back of a goods train at high speed at about 23:00 (21:00 GMT), said Belgium's rail infrastructure manager, Infrabel.The two trains were on the same track, a spokesman for Infrabel said.A local mayor said the passenger train was in a terrible state and it was fortunate there were no more victims.Francis Dejon, major of Saint-Georges-Sur-Meuse province, said the front carriage of the passenger train had completely folded back on itself.The train, with about 40 people on board, was travelling between Liege and Namur at about 90km/h (55mph) when it hit the goods train."Two of the six carriages derailed and are lying on the tracks," said a statement issued by Infrabel and national rail operator NMBS/SNCB.Some of those injured had to be freed from the wreckage and are in critical condition, officials say.Prime Minister Charles Michel tweeted his condolences to the families of the victims and wished the injured a speedy recovery.Infrabel has begun an investigation into the crash.There has been no comment as to why the trains were on the same track.Infrabel said there had been what it called a "lightning incident" on the track earlier on Sunday and a tweet by SNCB had mentioned a signalling fault on the line at 21:30 local time on Sunday, although it said this had been cleared and services were normal."The investigation into the cause of the rail crash is fully under way. The lightning strike is a possible line of inquiry, but there are still safety systems which are supposed to come into place," spokesman Frederic Petit told public broadcaster VRT.In February 2010, 18 people were killed when two passenger trains collided at Halle, south-west of the capital, Brussels.Two people died and 14 were hurt in May 2013 when a train carrying toxic, flammable chemicals derailed and caused a major fire near the city of Ghent.

State Attorney General Wayne Stenehjem has said more than one judge should have a chance to review the law.The law bans abortions after a foetal heartbeat is detected, as early as six weeks into pregnancy.In April, District Judge Daniel Hovland found the law "invalid and unconstitutional"."The United States Supreme Court has spoken and has unequivocally said no state may deprive a woman of the choice to terminate her pregnancy at a point prior to viability," Judge Hovland wrote in his ruling.The US Supreme Court ruled in the landmark Roe v Wade decision of 1973 that abortion must be legal until a foetus is viable, typically 22-24 weeks."The legislature passed the law in hopes that a higher court would revisit the issue," Mr Stenehjem said in a statement to the Associated Press news agency."It seems prudent that an appellate court should have an opportunity to consider the issue rather than have one judge overturn the judgment of the legislative assembly."The case will now be heard by the US 8th Circuit Court of Appeals.North Dakota's anti-abortion law was one of four such bills Republican Governor Jack Dalrymple signed into law in 2013.The state's sole abortion clinic and the Center for Reproductive Rights sued to overturn the law and a US district judge ruled in April."The court was correct to call this law exactly what it is - a blatant violation of the constitutional guarantees afforded to all women," Center for Reproductive Rights president Nancy Northrup wrote in a statement after the April ruling.

The Pollinating the Peak initiative aims to get volunteers to record sightings and help with the creation of bee habitats.The Bumblebee Conservation Trust said numbers "have crashed" in recent decades, with some species either threatened, or extinct.It said areas like the Peak District had suffered greatly due to a loss of habitat and farming practices.Trust chief executive Lucy Rothstein said: "Most of our pollinators are under threat, bumblebees in particular."One of the reasons is we've lost 98% of our wildflower meadows and that's why we are running the scheme in the Peak District."It is such an important place for wildflower habitats."She added: "The starting point is to understand where the bumblebees are in order to support our conservation work."The trust said out of 24 UK bumblebee species, two were extinct - with 18 in decline.Scientists believe that the destruction of their habitats - particularly wildflower meadows - has driven much of this loss.Research also suggests pesticides are playing a role in problem.The Moors for the Future partnership, which aims to tackle moorland erosion in the Peak District and South Pennines, is working alongside the trust to restore habitats.

In this study, we investigate the usefulness of specific color pairs as constraints for determining the age and metallicity of stellar populations. We utilize spectral stellar population synthesis models to analyze the effects of different color combinations on age and metallicity determinations. Through detailed simulations, we demonstrate that certain color pairs exhibit strong sensitivities to both age and metallicity variations.By considering the differences in color indices associated with varying ages and metallicities, we propose a set of color pairs that offer robust constraints on stellar population parameters. These color pairs are obtained from the analysis of Lick indices and composite colors derived from integrated spectral energy distributions.Furthermore, we evaluate the performance of these selected color pairs in comparison to other commonly used indicators, such as line indices and single colors. Our results indicate that these specific color pairs provide reliable estimations of stellar population ages and metallicities, showing significant advantages over alternative methods.The utility of these color pairs is further demonstrated through their application to real-world datasets, including star clusters M67 and NGC1868. The derived age and metallicity estimates based on the proposed color pairs exhibit good agreement with independent measurements, validating their effectiveness in constraining stellar population parameters.Overall, our study highlights the importance of color pairs as powerful tools for investigating the age and metallicity characteristics of stellar populations. The identified color pairs offer valuable insights into the evolutionary histories of galaxies and star clusters. They provide researchers with a practical and efficient approach to obtain accurate estimations of stellar population ages and metallicities, enabling more comprehensive studies of galaxy formation and evolution.

This study examines the atmospheric dynamics of short-period extra solar gas giant planets, with a particular focus on the relationship between night-side temperature and opacity. Using a combination of observational data and simulations, we investigate the effects of varying levels of opacity on the atmospheric circulation and temperature distribution of these planets. Our results show that increased opacity leads to a significant decrease in night-side temperature, particularly in the upper atmosphere. This cooling effect is found to be most pronounced in the vicinity of the terminator, where the transition from day to night side occurs. The observations suggest that the atmospheric dynamics of short-period gas giants are highly sensitive to changes in opacity, with important implications for our understanding of the atmospheric processes on these planets. Our findings have significant implications for the study of exoplanetary atmospheres and the search for potentially habitable worlds.

A friend of 16-year-old Morgan Huelin told a court he tried to call 999 as he lay unconscious the morning after a house party.The friend asked four others to help move him outside, Jersey Youth Court heard.The five teenagers deny perverting the course of justice in connection with Morgan's death.The friend, who can't be named for legal reasons, is the first of the boys to take to the stand.He had been friends with Mr Huelin at school, but more recently he'd distanced himself as Mr Huelin's drug taking "escalated".But after the party that night Mr Huelin asked if he could stay over and he didn't feel he could say no.The friend said he found Mr Huelin asleep in the garage the next day.He told the court: "I knew he'd been taking drugs. I didn't want my parents finding him in the state he was in."He asked his friends who were asleep in the house for help moving him down the road."I said to them, we needed to get him away from my parents, we needed to wake him up around the corner."When asked by Advocate Mike Preston if he tried to call for help, he said he had used a friend's phone to dial 999, but it wouldn't connect.After being asked why he didn't tell his parents, the teenager said: "I thought 999 was the safest way to deal with Morgan. I thought he would wake up, I didn't know it was so serious."Magistrate Bridget Shaw dismissed a request from the defence to throw out the case.The trial continues.
