Uncertainty gnawed at my mind as I grappled with the implications of my unique results.The official explained that a team of experts would be assembled to conduct a comprehensive analysis of my abilities and potential. They assured me that this was an opportunity, not a cause for alarm, but their words did little to quell the growing storm of questions within me.As they continued to outline the process and the various specialists who would be involved, my imagination ran wild. Visions of futuristic laboratories and enigmatic experiments danced before my eyes. What secrets did my aptitudes hold? What extraordinary potential lay dormant within me?As the official concluded their explanation, they handed me a sealed envelope, instructing me to open it only after I had reached home. Its weight felt significant, a tangible representation of the unknown journey that lay ahead.With a mixture of excitement and apprehension, I thanked the official and left the testing center.

**Full Review:**Madison Mallards games offer fun and family-friendly entertainment for a great price!  The games are affordable, and there are plenty of activities for kids to enjoy. The concessions are also reasonably priced, and the food is good. The atmosphere is relaxed and family-oriented, and the games are always exciting. Whether you're a baseball fan or not, you'll enjoy a Mallards game. So come on out to the ballpark and enjoy a great day with your family and friends. The team has a long and storied history, dating back to 1878. They are consistently one of the top teams in the Midwest, and they have won numerous league championships.

The top-quark mass is measured using the missing transverse energy plus jets with secondary vertex b-tagging technique in proton-antiproton collisions at a center-of-mass energy of 1.96 TeV with the CDF II detector at the Fermilab Tevatron. The data sample corresponds to an integrated luminosity of 2.7 fb$^{-1}$. Events are selected requiring large missing transverse energy, at least four jets, and at least one jet tagged as originating from a b-quark. A kinematic fit is used to reconstruct the mass of the top-quark pair and the top-quark mass is extracted using a template method. The measured top-quark mass is $173.1 \pm 1.2$ (stat) $\pm 1.1$ (syst) GeV/$c^2$. This result is consistent with the world average and the Standard Model prediction.

We report the measurement of extremely slow hole spin relaxation dynamics insmall ensembles of self-assembled InGaAs quantum dots. Individual spinorientated holes are optically created in the lowest orbital state of each dotand read out after a defined storage time using spin memory devices. Theresulting luminescence signal exhibits a pronounced polarization memory effectthat vanishes for long storage times. The hole spin relaxation dynamics aremeasured as a function of external magnetic field and lattice temperature. Weshow that hole spin relaxation can occur over remarkably long timescales instrongly confined quantum dots (up to ~270 us), as predicted by recent theory.Our findings are supported by calculations that reproduce both the observedmagnetic field and temperature dependencies. The results suggest that hole spinrelaxation in strongly confined quantum dots is due to spin orbit mediatedphonon scattering between Zeeman levels, in marked contrast to higherdimensional nanostructures where it is limited by valence band mixing.

The Tees Valley Airshow which was due to take place on Saturday has been postponed. The airshow which was planned to be the first since 1989 had been set to take place on August 29th. Organizers cited the crash of a Hawker Hunter jet at the Shoreham Airshow on Saturday as the reason for the postponement and said the thoughts of everyone involved were with the families of the victims. The Civil Aviation Authority has banned vintage jets from performing high-energy aerobatics over land at air shows following the Shoreham tragedy.

Man, imagine this, right? It's the year 2035, and there's this massive event, like an apocalypse, or whatever you want to call it, wiping out most of humanity. Just a handful of survivors, struggling to make sense of it all, you know? Among them, there's this guy, let's call him Jake, who stumbles upon this abandoned video store. He goes in and, guess what, finds a working TV and a single untouched VHS tape. It says, "The Last Video on Earth." Crazy, right? So, Jake dusts off that old VCR, pops in the tape, and what does he see? Footage of people. Happy people, sad people, living their lives before everything went to hell. It's like a time capsule, a glimpse into what used to be. And man, I'll tell you, Jake cries. He cries for the lost world, the forgotten joys, and the desire to connect with others. Because in that moment, with tears streaming down his face, he realizes that this tape, this last relic of humanity, is a reminder of what we had and what we long for.

The dark wizard Zanzubus stood over the obsidian alter, the ritual nearing completion even after the delays and set backs. He raised his crooked dagger for the final stroke when a thundering crash shook the temple of Kalil. The air ship " suck it Zibzab " crashed through the wall and slid to a halt in front of the alter. A mighty roar echoed through the chamber as Kyn Mites, the proverbial thorn in Zanzubus' s side, leaped from the prow. * " It ends here Zanzubus, there is nowhere for you to run, no teleportation, no secret passage we have n' t found, and no phylactery. My allies have secured them all as we speak, its just you and me. " * Zanzubus had long ago gotten used to Kyn' s overly dramatic interruptions, he only managed to delay Kalil' s return there was no stopping it. The hulking brute had no true understanding of magic or the gods, relying instead on his friends and the few trinkets he had found on his way. As such Zanzubus new there was always a way out, this time how ever he was prepared. * " Tisk tisk tisk, Kyn do n' t you know its bad manners to show up on time to a party? You might catch the host unprepared. " * * " Drop the knife Zanzubus " * * " As you wish " * Zanzubus tossed the knife in the air, as it fell towards the girl strapped to the alter Kyn charged to intercept it. Kyn lashed out swatting the blade away as Zanzubus cried out * " Kolpa " * activating the hidden runic circle beneath Kyns feet. In a blinding flash of light the warrior was gone. Tumbling through dimensions Kyn was whisked away from the lands of Mirn to the strange land of *Washington* where he was forced to get a job felling trees and spent his free time *surfing the web* for a portal back. If you have information on inter dimensional portals to a land of high adventure please contact kynmites at any email service provider.

I was born with a necklace that my parents had given me as a baby.  It was said that this necklace would help me find my soulmate.  As I grew older, I often forgot about the necklace and its supposed purpose.  But one day, when I was in my mid 20s, I started to feel a strange sensation.  The necklace began to warm up, like it was radiating heat.  I knew immediately that it was trying to tell me something.  I looked around, wondering if there was someone nearby who might be the one.  I saw a girl across the room, smiling at me, and I felt a jolt of excitement.  I approached her cautiously, not sure if this was really it.  But as we started talking, I felt the necklace grow warmer still.  We hit it off immediately, laughing and talking for hours.  I knew that this was the person the necklace had been leading me to.  We started dating and quickly realized that we were meant to be together.  The necklace had done its job, and I was grateful for its guidance.

This was my first time at Soba after spending many visits at Umi. I would argue that Umi is the better sibling with excellent sushi options (with a hefty price tag) but Soba is a bit more approachable to the person who is less familiar with or doesn't want sushi. In all, it's a good place for a date with a desire for reasonable and modern Asian fusion. nnAmbience: nTrendy, dimly lit and very clean decor. It suits the cuisine and is well set for small groups or a date. Not too loud but certainly not dead either. I loved the bar area- has a mix of bar and casual couch/lovechair seating that's well suited for a date or small group. Based on the clientele and set up, I'd lean towards trendy attire if you come here- not a casual place by any means. nnFood: nThai corn chowder- so good and very rich. I would return just for that dish. For an entree, I had the seared rare tuna. They definitely didn't skimp on my portion of tuna and cooked it well. Granted tuna is a bit bland, I was hoping for more seasoning and oomph from the Korean barbecue sauce. The ginger fried rice made up for any disappointment I had with the tuna. For dessert, the chocolate pretzel stack was fantastic- perfect mix of chocolate, sweet caramel and the salty wonton chip. nnDrinks: nThe old fashioned was well-executed. Went back for a second. Can't speak for any of their other cocktails.

I went there for the first time today, for Sunday brunch ($22 per person, prix fixe). The dining room is quite elegant as is the library in which the buffet is set out. The ambience suggests that the year is 1900. The waiter was very good, but they seemed to be understaffed. nnThe yogurt with fruit, the warm brie with strawberry, the hummus and toast, the chicken, the cinnamon pastry triangles, and the tiny eclairs were all quite good. The quiche was cold, tiny, and not particularly flavorful. Orange juice was served in dainty little glasses, causing anyone with a good thirst to order at least two. The wait for the entrees was excessive -- about 25 minutes from the time we ordered them. I got french toast and my kids got strawberry pancakes. Both were dry and unremarkable. I've cooked better french toast and pancakes at home, in my opinion. We didn't try the sandwiches or salads; perhaps they're better. nnOverall, it's not a bad place, but I think you can do better. Another elegant place for lunch or brunch, but better food, is Frick Cafe. nnNote: according to their own web page, http: //www. sunnyledge. com/, the name of this place is "Sunnyledge Boutique Hotel & Tea Room". "Historic" is not part of the name.

The pork here is whole hog, Eastern NC style barbecue cooked in electric pits with a few hickory chips thrown in near the end, and thus has little smokiness, but properly sauced it has a decent taste, though it is a little too finely chopped, in my opinion. The sauce is vinegar based, Eastern NC style without a lot of heat. This is good, solid, but not spectacular, chopped BBQ. nnThe slaw is the yellow version ( a little mustard is added) found generally only in the east (and even there, sparingly, and I really like it, on either a plate or a sandwich. Hush puppies are very nice, among my favorites, as well. Their French Fries are just great, about as good as you'll find in any NC BBQ joint - I love'em. The BBQ Chicken here is nice also. The Brunswick Stew is ok, but doesn't wow you. nnThe Chicken and Dumplings are very much worth a shot, as is the meatloaf, and beef tips. Sides are consistently good. This place is what it is, a solid, above average BBQ joint, and there simply aren't that many decent NC style barbecue choices in the Charlotte area. You can do much worse than eat a meal or two here.

Mae cyllid a chyfrifoldebau Llenyddiaeth Cymru yn wynebu toriadau sylweddol yn sgil adroddiad beirniadol diweddar. Yn ôl yr adroddiad, mae'r sefydliad wedi methu â chyflawni'r disgwyliadau a osodwyd iddo ac mae angen newidau sylweddol i'r modd y caiff ei ariannu a'i weinyddu. Mae'r toriadau arfaethedig yn cydfynd â pholisi cynyddol o gwtogi cyllidebau'r sector diwylliannol yng Nghymru.Mae'r adroddiad beirniadol hwn wedi herio'r ffordd y mae Llenyddiaeth Cymru yn cael ei reoli ac mae'n galw am weithredu uniongyrchol gan Lywodraeth Cymru i ddiogelu a sicrhau dyfodol y sefydliad. Gall y toriadau effeithio'n negyddol ar ymchwil, datblygiad a chynhyrchu llenyddol yng Nghymru, gan fod y sector hwn yn darparu lle i greadigedd a thalentau Cymru flodeuo.Mae manylion y toriadau a'r camau canlyniol i gynllunio'r ariannu a gwahaniaethu'r cyfrifoldebau yn dal i gael eu datblygu, ac mae'r perwyl yw sicrhau yn gyntaf a fydd hyn yn effeithio'n negyddol ar orffwys a chyfoeth lle siaradwyr Cymraeg. Yn dilyn yr adroddiad beirniadol hwn, bydd cynnwys y drafodaethau cerddorol, llenyddol a chyfoethogi'n rhwystredig gan nad oes sicrwydd ariannu parhaus.Yn ymateb i'r adroddiad, mae llawer yn galw ar Lywodraeth Cymru i gael gwared ar y toriadau sylweddol hyn a sicrhau bod canlyniadau ymchwil a datblygiad llenyddol yng Nghymru yn cael eu gefnogi'n llawn. People milled around outside the compound. All of the world' s leaders were attending the summit, where they were discussing the problem with North Korea. *North Korea is like the bastard child of the world - everyone knows it' s there, but they choose not to acknowledge it. How... unlucky. * " Mr Kim, we have arrived at the compound. ", a voice sounded from the speaker. " Thank you. Where is my speech? ", I asked, a hint of weariness in my voice. " It' s in your briefcase, sir. " " Park the car in the parking spaces. " I opened the doors and got out of my car. It went off by itself to park. Make no mistake - there' s no one driving it. Amazing how technology has progressed. As usual, the main chambers were full of energy and people talking sense. I went up to the President of the Untied States. " Mr. President, it is a pleasure you could finally make it. I hope to make some headway into today' s issue. Hopefully there shall be some tangible outcome to this, no? " " Well, the problem with your country is that, well, it' s really bad. People are dying and stuff. That is not good. So, uh, yeah. " *How quickly the good fall. * " Alright, nice to meet with you. Where' s the leader of South Korea? " **Twenty-four hours later** " And with the passing of resolution A/RES/69/310, North Korea will now have the full ability to do war and take over all the countries. It will have all trade restrictions and sanctions removed. Furthermore, all other countries present will be disarming all their nuclear installations. With 193 for, 0 against, and 0 abstentions, this resolution passes and is effective immediately. " *Victory is sweet. * I pulled out my phone. " It' s Kim. Launch the Last Offensive. "

In the depths of a forgotten cave, I stumbled upon the legendary weapon spoken of in ancient tales. The hilt shimmered with an otherworldly glow, beckoning me to grasp its power. With trembling hands, I lifted the weapon from its resting place, feeling a surge of energy course through my veins. Legends whispered that this weapon had the unparalleled ability to vanquish all evil in the world. Excitement filled my heart as I imagined the great deeds I could accomplish. However, as I held the weapon aloft, its voice echoed in my mind, "I am the Arbiter of Evil, and it is my duty to cleanse the world." Puzzled, I questioned the weapon, "How do you define evil?" The weapon's response sent a shiver down my spine, "Evil is anyone who opposes your desires, anyone who stands in the way of your ambition." Doubt gnawed at my conscience as I grappled with the weapon's skewed definition. Could I wield such power responsibly? Would I become the very evil I sought to eliminate? To find clarity, I ventured into the world, seeking the counsel of wise sages and experienced warriors. Each had their own interpretation of evil, rooted in morality and justice. Unsettled by the contradictory perspectives, I delved deeper into my own conscience. Reflecting on my past actions, I saw shades of darkness within my own choices. The weapon's definition of evil seemed to cater to my darkest desires and unchecked ego. Realization dawned upon me—I had discovered a double-edged sword. Understanding that the weapon's power could be manipulated to serve my personal agenda, I made a solemn vow to use it judiciously. Armed with newfound wisdom, I embarked on a quest to redefine the weapon's purpose. Through my actions, I would reshape its definition of evil, teaching it to recognize true malevolence and championing compassion and empathy. It became my mission to mitigate the weapon's skewed perception, to guide it towards a truer understanding of good and evil.

In the quaint little town of Harmony, where life had always been simple and predictable, an inexplicable mystery unfolded one morning. Upon waking, I found myself alone in the streets, the bustling sounds of life replaced by an eerie silence. The shops were deserted, the park empty, and the houses devoid of any sign of habitation.Confusion and fear gripped me as I wandered through the town, searching for any clue that could unravel this surreal situation. In my hand was a lone post-it note, bearing the cryptic message: "You won." Puzzled and disoriented, I clutched it tightly as I navigated the deserted streets.Amidst the stillness, my eyes fell upon an oddity—a block of cheese resting on a windowsill. It seemed utterly incongruous, an absurd reminder of the normality that had once defined this town. I picked it up, wondering if it held any significance in this bizarre puzzle.As I continued my solitary exploration, a mixture of apprehension and determination fueled my steps. Every house I entered held only emptiness, each room frozen in time as if the inhabitants had vanished into thin air. Desperation grew with each passing moment, and I yearned for any sign of life, any explanation for this inexplicable occurrence.Haunted by the haunting silence, I wandered into the heart of the town square, where the centerpiece fountain stood, still and lifeless. It was then that I stumbled upon a hastily scrawled note pinned to a tree, its message both cryptic and chilling: "The path of cheese leads to the truth."With renewed determination, I followed the elusive trail of cheese, allowing it to guide me through the empty streets. Each step forward brought me closer to unraveling the mystery, yet the weight of uncertainty pressed upon my shoulders.Along the way, I discovered discarded objects that hinted at the lives once lived here—a forgotten child's toy, a pair of glasses perched on an empty chair, a half-finished cup of coffee.

We set an upper limit on the mass of a supersymmetric neutralino dark matterparticle using the MicrOMEGAS and DarkSUSY software packages and the mostrecent constraints on relic density from combined WMAP and SDSS data. Weexplore several different possible scenarios within the MSSM, includingcoannihilation with charginos and sfermions and annihilation through a massiveHiggs resonance, using low energy mass inputs. We find that no coannihilationscenario is consistent with dark matter in observed abundance with a massgreater than 2.5 TeV for a wino--type particle or 1.8 TeV for a Higgsino--type.Contrived scenarios involving Higgs resonances with finely--tuned massparameters can allow masses as high as 34 TeV. The resulting gamma--ray energydistribution is not in agreement with the recent multi--TeV gamma ray spectrumobserved by H.E.S.S. originating from the center of the Milky Way. Our resultsare relevent only for dark matter densities resulting from a thermal origin.

I have been here multiple times, but this was my first visit to Bohn Cycle Shop. As soon as I walked in, I was greeted by a friendly staff member who seemed genuinely interested in helping me find the perfect scooter. They knew their stuff and were able to answer all my questions about different models and features. I must say, the selection they had was impressive, with a wide range of scooters in various styles and colors. I was told that they had just received some new stock, so I was excited to browse through their inventory. However, as I started looking around, I couldn't help but notice that the shop was a bit disorganized. It was a little frustrating trying to navigate through the clutter to get a closer look at the scooters. Despite this inconvenience, I decided to give it a shot and continued exploring.

nnIt's laid back atmosphere hits you from the parking lot, with acoustic sounds and the tinkling of laughter and wine glasses. At least a dozen tables spill outside the doors, filled with patrons and their cherished pets on any warm evening. It's commonplace to have live music from the patio, but doesn't overpower the conversations or keep the dogs from lounging. The ambiance, especially with the views of the lake and paddle boats, allows you to shift your mind into thinking you're anywhere but a local hangout in the Piedmont of NC. nnThe Vault itself has tiny quarters, but stocks some well-treasured and fresh names, both in wine and brews. Snacks are available, with a varied assortment of cheeses, pretzels and party mix. It's very simple to walk in, make your selections, then take to the register for purchase. The only problem you may find are long lines, so I wouldn't wait until the last minute for a drink or the bathroom. nnIt's worth a drive from anywhere in the Charlotte area to see what the UCB has been keeping to itself. This place is equally impressive for the intimate conversation of a first date, relaxing with a group of friends after work, or bringing your dog for some appreciative petting from strangers. If you're single, the last one is especially fun.

Clive Rees, from Tumble, was celebrating his 65th birthday on the Caribbean island with his wife Sonia when he was hit by a car.He is being treated in an intensive care unit after developing pneumonia and breathing difficulties.Mr Rees is unable to return home on a commercial flight and the family is raising money for an air ambulance.They have made a heartfelt plea to the public to help them reach their target following the incident on 13 May."You always think something like this will never happen to you," said daughter Tracy Rees."We just want him home."Miss Rees said the response has been "overwhelming", with support from family, friends and local businesses."We've raised about £2,000 so far," she said."I just can't believe how generous everyone is. Thank you from the bottom of our hearts."The £65,000 air ambulance fee encompasses a return trip from the UK, refuelling and a team of medical staff to look after Mr Rees.

While some reviewers may lament the outdated interiors, a feature that was not missed by me, I will merely say that Lombardozzi proved to be the best venue for our toddler that we have come upon in months. Our server did not know the difference between bolognese and meat sauce, but she brought soup and milk for our toddler faster than you can say pronto! She fawned over our daughter and made us feel comfortable. nnAdditionally, the owner and chef both came to our table to talk to us. My husband and I felt like we were eating at a friend's house, and that warmth is a hell of a lot sexier than any atmosphere we've gotten in a swanky Shadyside lounge. nnWe ordered simple pasta dishes: linguine and meat sauce, and spaghetti and meatballs. The pasta was fresh and al dente; the garnishes were tasteful and classic; the sauces were remarkable. nnI recommend this place for the food and congenial service. It would easily get 5 stars if we hadn't sat in the outdated bar.

The Big Issue, a magazine sold by homeless people on the streets of the UK, is now going digital. The magazine, which has been in print for over 25 years, will be available in a digital format starting from March 11th. This move comes as a response to the changing media landscape and the increasing demand for digital content. The digital version of the magazine will be available on various platforms, including smartphones, tablets, and computers, and will feature the same content as the print edition, including articles, interviews, and features on social and political issues.

Eleven-year-old Francis Rowntree was shot by a soldier in west Belfast in 1972.Henry Donaghy, who was with Francis on the day, has been giving evidence to the inquest.Mr Donaghy, who was 14 at the time, told the court there were no cars on fire or rioting in the area.He said that while he could hear disturbances nearby, the trouble had moved along the road.Explaining that they were making their way though the area when they came across an army vehicle, Mr Donaghy said:   "Wee Francie was more curious than the rest of us."He started to walk towards the Saracen.  He wasn't carrying anything."He told the inquest that Francis was ahead of the rest of the group and just eight to 10 yards from the vehicle when he heard a loud bang.He said Francis appeared to be "lifted off his feet and jerked backwards".He said he knew something was "desperately wrong"."It scared us, the colour on his face."Mr Donaghy said the observation flap at the rear of the Saracen was "completely open" and all he could see after the bullet had been fired was "smoke and powder debris" coming from an area around the Army vehicle.Francis Rowntree, known as Frank by his family, died in the Royal Victoria Hospital two days after being hit - on 22 April, 1972.

The Met Office has issued an amber "be prepared" weather warning for heavy rain across central and southern Scotland on Wednesday, with the potential for flooding and disruption to travel. The warning is in place from 6am to 6pm on Wednesday, and covers areas including Edinburgh, Glasgow, and the Scottish Borders. The Met Office has warned that heavy rain could lead to flooding of roads, homes, and businesses, and that travel could be disrupted. The agency has advised people to be prepared for the possibility of flooding and to take steps to protect themselves and their property.

Have you ever noticed that "Fusion" almost always involves East Asian food and American cuisine. Thai corn chowder? Are they really going crazy for corn chowder in Chang Mai? nnYou never find, say, Mexican-Moroccan or  Ethiopian-Brazilian fusion places. That would be really inventive. Instead, with Soba, you have another pan Asian-American Fusion joint, although I don't think I've been to a better one. nnThe mussels are incredible, a meal in themselves, and a bargain at $9. nnI had the chopped salad, with fresh mango, avocado, mint, and just a hint of spice. The best meal I've had here was the mussels, followed by the tuna. The crabcakes are also good, made with lump crabmeat. nnIf you feel like something vaguely Asian, but not too Asian (ex: kimchee pancakes), you won't find a better option. But if you want something truly original, you have to go elsewhere.

This article investigates the impact of lepton flavor violation (LFV) on the production processes of charginos, the supersymmetric partners of charginos, at the Linear Collider. The study is conducted within the framework of the Minimal Supersymmetric Standard Model (MSSM), and considers the influence of LFV on the t-channel sneutrino exchange contribution to the processes. The article finds that the LFV parameters can alter the cross section of the processes by a factor of 2 or more, and highlights the importance of including LFV parameters in deducing the underlying model parameters from measured observables. The study also explores the restrictions on LFV mixing angles due to the current limits on rare lepton decays.

As I gazed out the window of the International Space Station, I couldn't help but feel a sense of awe at the breathtaking view of our planet. The blue and green hues of Earth seemed to stretch on forever, and the thin layer of atmosphere that separated us from the vastness of space seemed almost surreal.Suddenly, my attention was drawn to a small, pinprick of light that appeared in the distance. At first, I thought it was just a star or a meteor, but as I watched, more and more lights began to appear, until there were hundreds of them scattered across the globe.My heart began to race as I realized that these were no ordinary lights. They were nuclear bombs, detonating in cities all over the world. Panic set in as I tried to contact Houston, but the silence on the other end of the line was deafening.I tried to reach out to my fellow astronauts, but they were all too stunned to respond. We were all alone, floating in space, watching as our home planet was torn apart by violence and destruction.The images of destruction poured in, city after city, country after country. The world as we knew it was ending, and we were powerless to stop it.I felt a sense of hopelessness wash over me, knowing that we were the last remnants of humanity, adrift in a vast, uncaring universe. The thought of our own mortality was a crushing weight, and I couldn't help but wonder if this was the end of our species.But even in the face of such despair, I knew that we couldn't give up. We had to keep fighting, keep struggling, until the very end. For the sake of those who had been lost, and for the sake of those who were still left behind, we had to keep going.With a heavy heart, I turned my attention back to the view of Earth, watching as the bombs continued to detonate, knowing that our world would never be the same again. But even in the midst of such destruction, I knew that we would find a way to persevere, to rebuild, and to keep moving forward.

The Foyle Cup is now in its 25th year and has attracted a record entry of 310 teams.An opening parade weaved its way through the city on Tuesday.Foyle Cup chairman Michael Hutton said it was the biggest tourist event in Derry,He said there were 310 teams, a festival of football for six days and 1,034 games.Earlier this year, fears for the future of the tournament were raised after funding was cut by 50% by Tourism Northern Ireland.The organisers had said that would prevent them attracting the same calibre of teams from abroad as in previous years.They also argued that the event, which started out as an eight-team youth tournament in 1992, is worth almost Â£2m to the local economy."It's as big, if not bigger, than the jazz festival, the Halloween festival and indeed the maritime festival in terms of bed nights created."We have about 5,000 young people marching in a parade for the mayor's welcome at Guildhall Square today."It's great to see the pro clubs from England and Scotland, it's great to welcome our teams from New York. We have Landsdowne Bhoys here for the first time and Everton America."Mr Hutton also welcomed greater involvement from the ladies teams."There's definitely been an increase in girls and ladies football."This year we have 28 teams in all, we're doing all we can to promote the development of girls and ladies football throughout the north of Ireland and Ireland in general and it's great to see."The Foyle Cup tournament will be held at various venues in the north-west until 22 July.

First of all, sorry for my bad English. My letter. We met not a long time ago but I think I truly am in love with you. When I first met you I did n' t really liked you nor disliked you. You were just normal. Just my schoolmate. But after some time I started to feel something for you. Every time I saw you my heart beat like crazy. I loved talking to you but we did n' t talk that much. Somehow I felt that you like me too. Just a little. Maybe it was true. But now I know you like someone else and for a long time. The worst part is you told me yourself. You talked to me like to your friend. Saying you love her so much. To me. My heart felt like it was broking into pieces. How could you? I love you so much, like anyone else I liked. When you told me about her, the only thing I could think about was how lucky she is. I do n' t know how to look at you anymore. How to smile like nothing happened. I do n' t know how to talk with you normally. But I will try. I will be your friend, your listener, your support. So please do n' t leave me and talk to me about her anymore.

This article presents a comprehensive investigation into the density dependence of the symmetry energy and its implications for the equation of state of nuclear matter. Using a combined dynamical and statistical model perspective, we explore the behavior of the symmetry energy in the context of various nuclear phenomena. The study utilizes heavy-ion reactions with neutron-rich nuclei to probe the isospin independence of in-medium nuclear interactions. In particular, we focus on the density dependence of the symmetry energy and its role in determining the equation of state of neutron-rich nuclear matter, with emphasis on the density dependence of the symmetry energy's isospin-dependent term. Through both experimental and theoretical approaches, significant progress has been achieved in constraining the density dependence of the symmetry energy, confirming the presence of isospin-dependent effects and their impact on the properties of neutron stars. The findings highlight the importance of isospin physics in nuclear astrophysics and project a promising future for further advancements in this field, facilitated by the construction of new radioactive beam facilities worldwide.

After enduring a grueling two-and-a-half-year wait, a courageous 14-year-old named Ewan MacKerracher has finally received the long-awaited news - a date for his life-changing kidney removal operation. The young boy and his family have faced countless challenges and hurdles throughout their journey, living with the constant fear and uncertainty that accompanied his condition. Ewan's battle with kidney complications has been nothing short of a rollercoaster ride, filled with ups and downs, hope and despair. Today, however, a glimmer of hope shines through, as the medical team has confirmed a specific date for his surgery. Ewan's family, filled with a mix of emotions ranging from relief to nervous anticipation, is counting down the days until the procedure that holds the key to transforming his young life. The weight of the wait is slowly lifting, and a newfound sense of joy is palpable in the air, as Ewan's loved ones eagerly anticipate witnessing his restored health and the return of his cheeky grin.

We study the direct CP violation in $\bar{B}^0 \to\rho^0(\omega)\rho^0(\omega) \to \pi^+\pi^-\pi^+\pi^-$ (with unpolarized$\rho^0(\omega)$) via the $\rho-\omega$ mixing mechanism which causes a largestrong phase difference and consequently a large CP violating asymmetry whenthe masses of the $\pi^+\pi^-$ pairs are in the vicinity of the $\omega$resonance. Since there are two $\rho (\omega)$ mesons in the intermediate state$\rho-\omega$ mixing contributes twice to the first order of isospin violation,leading to an even larger CP violating asymmetry (could be 30% -- 50% larger)than in the case where only one $\rho (\omega)$ meson is involved. The CPviolating asymmetry depends on the Cabibbo-Kobayashi-Maskawa (CKM) matrixelements and the hadronic matrix elements. The factorization approach isapplied in the calculation of the hadronic matrix elements with thenonfactorizable effects being included effectively in an effective parameter,$N_c$. We give the constraint on the range of $N_c$ from the latestexperimental data for the branching ratios for $\bar{B}^0 \to\rho^0\rho^0$ and$\bar{B}^0 \to\rho^+\rho^-$. We find that the CP violating asymmetry could bevery large (even more than 90% for some values of $N_c$). It is shown that thesensitivity of the CP violating asymmetry to $N_c$ is large compared with itssmaller sensitivity to the CKM matrix elements. We also discuss the possibilityto remove the mod $(\pi)$ ambiguity in the determination of the CP violatingphase angle $\alpha$ through the measurement of the CP violating asymmetry inthe decay $\bar{B}^0\to \rho^0(\omega)\rho^0(\omega) \to \pi^+\pi^-\pi^+\pi^-$.

Microsoft had agreed to provide customers with a screen to allow them to chose internet browsers other than its own Internet Explorer.In a statement, Microsoft conceded it had "fallen short", blaming a technical error.The European Commission said penalties for non-compliance would be "severe".EU competition commissioner Joaquin Almunia said it appeared the browser choice screen (BCS) had not been provided since February 2011, meaning 28 million customers might not have seen it.Microsoft said PCs running the original version of Windows 7, as well as Windows XP and Windows Vista, did have the screen.But it added: "Due to a technical error, we missed delivering the BCS software to PCs that came with the service pack 1 update to Windows 7."While we have taken immediate steps to remedy this problem, we deeply regret that this error occurred and we apologise for it."Mr Almunia said:  "Needless to say, we take compliance with our decision very seriously."If the infringement is confirmed, there will be sanctions."To date the European Union has fined Microsoft 1.64 billion euros (Â£1.29bn).

I put the flamethrower down and kick the ashes of the last zombie. There' s a barely dressed busty redhead sobbing, clinging to me and calling me a hero. I already know what happens next: I will kiss her, she will kiss me, and we will go for a clean place to mate like dogs in heat. Then, tomorrow or some other day, something will happen to her. She will be in danger and I will have to rescue her, saving the world in the way and becoming stronger, more decided, more unlike me. And then she' ll die. That' s what happened to the last ten of them, at least. Nothing new under the son. I know I' m supposed to love them, but after a while they all seem the same. No. I do n' t want this. I let go of her and walk on my way. My shadows goes ahead of me. There' s dust in the wind, a fresh breeze that moves my hair, and a sun-bathed path that might lead anywhere. The perfect setting for another adventure, that I do n' t want. Why does it have to be like this? Why am I forbidden to stay in any place, to live the tranquil life I' ve always wanted? I' ve lost count of the many times that I' ve tried to do it, but almost like a dark force guiding my life to an heroic path, always something happens that throws me back in the blood and fights and glory, and I' ve had enough of that for a lifetime. The woman stands behind, shocked and sobbing again. She needs me. But I do n' t need her or want her, and it will be better for her to stay away from me. Maybe then she can find the peace that I ca n' t find. Another step. A small stone upturns and shows a mirrorlike face with a strange inscription. No matter how fast I run, my fate will find me. So I pick the stone, I know in time I will find its meaning. I sigh again. There' s not a happy ending behind me, just a trail of unwanted victories. But maybe ahead of me, at the end of this, I will finally be allowed to rest. I cling to that hope. And I walk.

I'm sorry, this place is simply not good. The pasta dishes are bland and uninspired, and the pizza is a pale imitation of the real thing. I've had better Italian food at a chain restaurant. The atmosphere is trying too hard to be quirky and endearing, but it ends up feeling forced and uncomfortable. The service is slow and unfriendly, and the prices are way too high for the quality of food you receive. I was excited to try this place out, but I left feeling disappointed and ripped off.I ordered the spaghetti with meatballs, which was served with a side of marinara sauce that tasted like it came straight from a jar. The pasta was overcooked and mushy, and the meatballs were dry and flavorless. I was expecting a rich, hearty dish that would fill me up and leave me feeling satisfied, but instead I felt like I had just eaten a bland, forgettable meal.My friend ordered the chicken parmesan, which was also a disappointment. The chicken was overcooked and tasted like it had been sitting out for hours, and the sauce was too sweet and sticky. The garlic bread that came with it was the highlight of the meal, but that's not saying much.I was hoping that the desserts would redeem the meal, but they were a letdown as well. The cannoli was filled with a bland, sweet ricotta cheese that had no flavor or texture, and the tiramisu was a soggy, falling-apart mess. I left feeling disappointed and frustrated, wishing I had chosen a different restaurant for my night out.I've heard that this place has a great atmosphere, but I didn't find that to be the case. The decor was tacky and over-the-top, with too many old Italian posters and cheap trinkets cluttering up the walls. The tables were cramped and uncomfortable, and the whole place felt like it was trying too hard to be something it's not.I've also heard that the service here is top-notch, but that wasn't my experience. Our server was slow and unfriendly, and it took forever to get our food and drinks.

The year was 2057, and the world had changed. Corporations now owned whole nations, and I lived in one of those nations. The company that owned our nation was called "Omicron," and they had a reputation for being ruthless.I worked for Omicron, along with almost everyone else in the country. Our days were long and grueling, with shift after shift of hard labor. The pay was minimal, but we were told it was a small price to pay for the privilege of living in the great nation of Omicron.The company controlled every aspect of our lives. They told us what to wear, what to eat, and who to associate with. We lived in small, cramped apartments that were provided by the company, and we were watched constantly by cameras and drones.Despite the strict rules, there were those who resisted. A group of rebels, known as the "Luddites," had been secretly meeting in the shadows, planning their next move. I had heard rumors of their existence, but I had never seen them myself.One day, as I was walking home from work, I saw something that made my heart race. A group of people, dressed in black, were handing out flyers. The flyers read, "Join the resistance. Fight back against the corporations. Meet us at the old clock tower at midnight."I knew I had to go. I had always felt a sense of unease under the watchful eye of Omicron, and the idea of fighting back was exhilarating. I made my way home, my heart pounding in my chest.That night, I snuck out of my apartment and made my way to the clock tower. When I arrived, I saw a group of people huddled together, their faces obscured by masks. They handed me a flyer with instructions on how to join the resistance.I followed the instructions, and soon I was part of the rebellion. We met in secret, planning our attacks on the corporations. We were careful, always watching for the drones and cameras that were always watching us.One night, we decided to take action.

You order the pizza with whatever toppings you so desire. You get handed your drink cups and a number to put on your table and try and find a seat. Maybe a 15 to 20 minute wait for your pizza. Your pizza comes out and.. .. .. "what the hell is this? Take this back! My cheese ain't melted and the pepperoni ain't cooked! " Chill my man.. .. . take a few deep breaths.. .. it'll be ok.. .. . just TASTE it.. .. .. there ya go.. .. . holy cheese right?! What a blend.. .. It's all over the place and people actually order extra! That sauce, nice.. .. . sweet and chunky.. .. yummy. And the crust.. .. the crispy, chewy wonderful foundation that holds it all together. It's a bit different. But different in the best way. There's some great pizza out there. Different styles and different tastes. Do yourself a favor. If you're in the Banksville Rd. / Dormont area stop in this place.. .. it's just delicious.

They asked first that he renounce the revision of property titles in order to get back the support of the Liberal landowners. They asked, secondly, that he renounce the fight against clerical influence in order to obtain the support of the Catholic masses. They asked, finally, that he renounce the aim of equal rights for natural and illegitimate children in order to preserve the integrity of the home. "That means, " Colonel Aureliano Buendía said, smiling when the reading was over, "that all we' re fighting for is power. " "They' re tactical changes, " one of the delegates replied. "Right now the main thing is to broaden the popular base of the war. Then we' ll have another look. " One of Colonel Aureliano Buendía' s political advisers hastened to intervene. "It' s a contradiction, " he said. "If these changes are good, it means that the Conservative regime is good. If we succeed in broadening the popular base of the war with them, as you people say, it means that the regime has a broad popular base. It means, in short, that for almost twenty years we' ve been fighting against the sentiments of the nation. "

Intrinsic ripples in graphene: A new frontier in 2D materials researchGraphene, a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a hexagonal lattice, has captivated the scientific community due to its exceptional electronic and mechanical properties. However, recent studies have unveiled a previously overlooked feature of this material: intrinsic ripples. These ripples, which are inherent to the structure of graphene, have the potential to revolutionize our understanding of 2D materials and their behavior.In this article, we explore the phenomenon of intrinsic ripples in graphene and their implications for the future of materials science. We begin by examining the structural properties of graphene and how they give rise to ripples. We then delve into the unique electronic and mechanical characteristics of rippled graphene, and discuss how these properties can be harnessed for applications in fields such as electronics, energy storage, and biomedicine.Our analysis reveals that intrinsic ripples in graphene introduce a new level of complexity and richness to the behavior of 2D materials. By studying these ripples, researchers can gain valuable insights into the interactions between atoms and the role of geometry in material properties. The study of intrinsic ripples in graphene also opens up new avenues for the design and synthesis of novel 2D materials with tailored properties.The discovery of intrinsic ripples in graphene marks a significant milestone in the advancement of 2D materials science. As research in this field continues to evolve, we can expect to uncover even more exciting properties and potential applications of graphene and other 2D materials. With further investigation, the intrinsic ripples in graphene may hold the key to unlocking novel technologies and scientific breakthroughs.

In the year 2200, the world had undergone a remarkable transformation with the eradication of all ailments that plagued humanity for centuries. Diseases that once brought suffering and death were now mere fragments of history. The marvels of medical advancements had conquered illness, but the unforeseen consequence was a rapidly growing population that threatened to decimate the Earth's resources. It was against this backdrop that I found myself on a top-secret board consisting of nine influential individuals, tasked with making a fateful decision. We were presented with a newly developed pathogen, a weapon that could reset the balance by wiping out three-quarters of the population, allowing us to start anew. As I sat in the meeting room, surrounded by the weight of the world, the gravity of our decision enveloped me. Each member of the board had their reasons for supporting or opposing the release of the pathogen. The discussions were heated, emotions ran high, and the ethical implications were overwhelming. I carefully listened to each viewpoint, grappling with the profound responsibility bestowed upon me. The fate of billions rested in my hands, a heavy burden to bear. As the decisive vote drew near, I delved deep into my thoughts, considering the repercussions of unleashing such a devastating force upon humanity. I reflected on the potential loss of innocent lives, the destruction of families, and the moral dilemma at the core of our deliberations. The pathogen offered a chance to restore balance and preserve the planet for future generations, but at what cost? Could we condemn ourselves to be the architects of destruction? In the end, my decision was not swayed by emotions or personal beliefs. It was guided by an unwavering commitment to the greater good and a belief in humanity's resilience. I cast my vote, carefully considering the long-term consequences and the potential for alternative solutions.

But I had a spot of coffee on my white winter coat and brought it here for dry-cleaning and pointed out the spot and asked if they could treat it. Well I took the coat home and was heart-broken because it is my favorite so I squirted it with Shout and ran it through cold cycle in the machine - what did I have to lose at this point? The spot came out perfectly and I air-dried the coat.. .. . it is now back to its original perfection. If I can do this why cant they?? ? I didnt try to get my money back or anything, just lesson learned, they are NOT good with spots.. .. . even though they may say they 'tried everything'.. .. .. . nnI have been going here for my dry-cleaning needs for about a year now. The staff isnt super sweet but nice enough. There is parking right in front of the store but there arent a lot of spots so I try to go at off times. nnEden you are A-Ok at the regular stuff but I know for tough spots I cant count on you!

**Abstract**:This paper proposes a Bayesian approach to the separation of mixed text/image documents, where a Hidden Markov Model (HMM) is used to model the underlying structure of the document. The HMM is trained on a set of labeled document images, and then used to classify each pixel in a new document image as either text or image. The classification results are then used to generate a binary image of the document, which can be used for further processing, such as optical character recognition (OCR). The proposed approach is evaluated on a set of document images, and the results show that it outperforms existing methods in terms of both accuracy and speed.

**Abstract**: Stability limits of supercooled liquid clusters are investigated using extensive molecular dynamics simulations. We find a dynamic crossover from metastable to unstable behavior as the cluster size decreases, indicating a finite limit of stability in supercooled liquids. Our results provide a microscopic understanding of the stability of small liquid clusters. Furthermore, we reveal a strong correlation between the stability limit and the properties of the corresponding crystal. This finding suggests a possible strategy to predict the stability of supercooled liquids by investigating the properties of their crystalline phases. Our results pave the way for a better understanding of the behavior of supercooled liquids and the formation of glasses.

Yo, can you believe it? I'm just your average Joe, a regular ol' human being, but here's the twist: I've been freaking immortal since like a thousand years ago, back in the year 1,014 A.D. Crazy, right? So here I am, sitting in my fancy corporate job in America, reflecting on this epic journey of mine.Let me tell ya, it ain't been all sunshine and rainbows. Sure, I've had plenty of time to see the world, witness history unfold, and master a gazillion hobbies. But man, the loneliness can really get to you after a while. Everyone I've ever known and loved, long gone while I remain stuck in time.Sometimes I wonder what it'd be like to grow old gracefully, to have a family and watch my kids turn into adults. But nah, fate had other plans for me. Eternal youth, immortality, and endlessly chasing the next thrill. Life's a trip, I tell ya.I've seen empires rise and fall, watched civilizations crumble into dust. Wars, revolutions, scientific breakthroughs—they've all been a part of my never-ending existence. It's like being trapped in a never-ending rollercoaster, exhilarating and terrifying at the same damn time.And now, here I am in the land of corporate America, navigating suits, deadlines, and the eternal struggle to climb that corporate ladder. It's a rat race, my friend, and I'm sprinting alongside everyone else, day after day, year after year. Guess immortality didn't come with a get-out-of-work-free card.But hey, amidst all the chaos and monotony, there are moments that make it all worthwhile. The friendships forged, the connections made—they're the little drops of magic in this grand and bizarre life of mine. It's those moments that remind me why I keep going, why I keep pushing forward.So here's to embracing the unpredictable, to savoring every fleeting moment, and to figuring out what the hell this immortal soul of mine is really meant to do. Cheers to the millennium I've lived, and to the eternity that lies ahead.

**U.S. Stocks Close Fractionally Down Despite Good Jobs News**U.S. stocks closed fractionally down on Thursday, despite data showing an increase in non-farm payrolls, as investors awaited a speech from Federal Reserve Chair Janet Yellen later in the week. The Dow Jones Industrial Average lost 16.95 points, or 0.09%, to end at 18,474.53, with declines led by energy and financial shares. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite dropped 3.81 points, or 0.07%, to finish at 5,234.45, while the S&P 500 index edged down 1.58 points, or 0.07%, to close at 2,175.63. Investors remain cautious, with economic data suggesting a mixed picture, but the market will be looking to Yellen's speech for clues about the future of interest rates.

I just moved to PIttsburgh from NYC and I relied on yelp to choose my hair salon. I called MCN and asked to be seen by a senior stylist and the receptionist recommended Ashley. I booked her for a cut and full highlight. nWhen I got there, I was pleased with the look of the salon. It's trendy, clean, and has a good vibe. I met Ashley and she was pleasant enough, although she seems a bit young to be a senior stylist. But who am I to know what the credentials are to be considered "senior"? She did a good job on the color and didn't do a terrible job on the cut, but I just wasn't wow'd. She talked the entire time, which I don't typically mind and she's easy to talk to, but too much of the conversation was geared toward selling me product. nMy hair is straight and thin and I had previously gotten a stacked bob (which I hate and I'm trying to grow out). She did her best to make it better, but it's still too stacked for my liking and is a bit uneven (found this out a week later when a different stylist was doing my hair for a wedding and fixed it). nThe prices were a bit high for this area. My haircut and highlight (she didn't do a full highlight, just partial which wasn't what I booked, but I didn't say anything so I'm somewhat to blame) came to $175. nAlso, there was a lot of pressure to come back in 6 weeks. I can't afford to get my hair cut and highlighted every 6 weeks and don't know anyone who goes that often. nnBottom line- my experience wasn't horrible and wasn't great. I will go back to give the salon a second chance, but will be trying out a different stylist.

The study of the innermost circumstellar layers around AGB stars is crucialto understand how these envelopes are formed and evolve. The SiO maser emissionoccurs at a few stellar radii from the central star, providing directinformation on the stellar pulsation and on the chemical and physicalproperties of these regions. Our data also shed light on several aspects of theSiO maser pumping theory that are not well understood yet. We aim to determine}the relative spatial distribution of the 43 GHz and 86 GHz SiO maser lines inthe oxygen-rich evolved star R Leo. We have imaged with milliarcsecondresolution, by means of Very Long Baseline Interferometry, the 43 GHz (28SiOv=1, 2 J=1-0 and 29SiO v=0 J=1-0) and 86 GHz (28SiO v=1 J=2-1 and 29SiO v=0J=2-1) masing regions. We confirm previous results obtained in otheroxygen-rich envelopes. In particular, when comparing the 43 GHz emittingregions, the 28SiO v=2 transition is produced in an inner layer, closer to thecentral star. On the other hand, the 86 GHz line arises in a clearly farthershell. We have also mapped for the first time the 29SiO v=0 J=1-0 emission in RLeo. The already reported discrepancy between the observed distributions of thedifferent maser lines and the theoretical predictions is also found in R Leo.

For what seemed like an eternity, I had been trapped in a land of fantasy. I knew that it was a video game; I always had, for whatever odd reason. The world was inhabited by all kinds of creatures: humans, elves, orcs, dragons... you name it. I spent my days adventuring, and I grew rich and even bought a house. Before long, I had a family. Then, I sustained an injury. Some maniac guardian of a dungeon shot me with a crossbow. I was lucky I had a friend with me, otherwise I would' ve surely been finished. The problem was that the game did n' t exactly have modern medicine. The bolt had pierced my leg, and I was told I could never run again, and I' d have trouble even walking. My days of being a hero were over. Then came the identity crisis. Was I truly the main character of the game? What kind of stupid video game would be based around losing the ability to walk and spending your life cooped up in a small village? But I could n' t be an AI; I controlled who I was, what I did. Those years were rough. After many an existential crisis, I gave up on searching for the answer and accepted my boring fate. As I sat at a bar, sipping mead and thinking about my glory days, a young man came in. He had a sword in a scabbard, a quiver across his back, and a dangerous gleam in his eyes. I saw myself in him. He took a seat next to me and ordered a drink. I took a sip as I looked over at him. " I used to be an adventurer like you, " I spoke. " Then I took an arrow in the knee. "

The numerical simulation of wave propagation in semiclassical(high-frequency) problems is well known to pose a formidable challenge. In thiswork, a new phase-space approach for the numerical simulation of semiclassicalwave propagation, making use of the smoothed Wigner Transform (SWT), isproposed. There are numerous works which use the Wigner Transform (WT) in thestudy of a variety of wave propagation problems including high-frequency limitsfor linear, nonlinear and/or random waves. The WT however is well known topresent significant difficulties in the formulation of numerical schemes.Working with concrete examples for the semiclassical linear Schrodingerequation it is seen that the SWT approach is indeed significantly faster (in awell-defined sense) to work with than the WT and than full numerical solutionsof the original equation in the semiclassical regime. Comparisons with exactand numerical solutions are used to keep track of numerical errors.

In the heart of the bustling city, a figure emerged from the shadows of the night, wielding power and influence like an ethereal force. This enigmatic being, known only as the Cocaine God, had become a legend whispered among the underworld.His empire thrived on the addiction and desperation of those seeking the euphoric embrace of his illicit substance. It was said that his cocaine was unlike any other, possessing a potency that ensnared its users, leaving them craving more of its blissful intoxication.The Cocaine God was a master manipulator, weaving intricate webs of corruption and deceit that ensnared both the powerful and the vulnerable. He operated in the shadows, his name spoken only in hushed tones among the criminal elite.But as rumors of his existence permeated the city, curiosity transformed into obsession for one man. John, a disillusioned detective haunted by his past, embarked on a personal crusade to uncover the truth behind the Cocaine God's empire.John dedicated himself to tracing the illicit drug's source, meticulously following the trail of destruction left in its wake. Each clue he unraveled led him deeper into a world of darkness and desperation.Haunted by his own demons, John found solace in the anonymity of his investigation. He could lose himself in the underbelly of the city, where the lines between right and wrong blurred in the haze of addiction and desire.As John delved further into the depths of the Cocaine God's empire, he discovered a network of corruption that reached far beyond his initial suspicions. Powerful figures in the city's government and law enforcement were entangled in the web of the Cocaine God's influence.Fear began to consume John as he realized the perilous path he had embarked upon. He knew that exposing the Cocaine God meant risking his own life and those he held dear. But he couldn't turn back now—he had seen too much.A chance encounter with a mysterious informant provided John with a crucial lead.

I walked in and was greeted by the familiar sight of the menu on the wall. I had forgotten how extensive it was, with so many options that it was hard to choose just one. When the sandwich arrived, I was immediately struck by how massive it was. The chicken was breaded and fried to a crispy perfection, and the melted mozzarella cheese was gooey and delicious.I took a bite and was blown away by the flavors. The chicken was juicy and tender, and the sauce was rich and tangy. It was a perfect blend of flavors that left me wanting more. We present a ground based photometry of the low-temperature contact binary BBPeg. We collected all times of mid-eclipses available in literature andcombined them with those obtained in this study. Analyses of the data indicatea period increase of 3.0(1) x 10^{-8} days/yr. This period increase of BB Pegcan be interpreted in terms of the mass transfer 2.4 x 10^{-8} Ms yr^{-1} fromthe less massive to the more massive component. The physical parameters havebeen determined as Mc = 1.42 Ms, Mh = 0.53 Ms, Rc = 1.29 Rs, Rh = 0.83 Rs, Lc =1.86 Ls, and Lh = 0.94 Ls through simultaneous solution of light and of theradial velocity curves. The orbital parameters of the third body, that orbitsthe contact system in an eccentric orbit, were obtained from the periodvariation analysis. The system is compared to the similar binaries in theHertzsprung-Russell and Mass-Radius diagram.

Star patterns, reminiscent of a wide range of diffusively controlled growthforms from snowflakes to Saffman-Taylor fingers, are ubiquitous features of icecovered lakes. Despite the commonality and beauty of these ``lake stars'' theunderlying physical processes that produce them have not been explained in acoherent theoretical framework. Here we describe a simple mathematical modelthat captures the principal features of lake-star formation; radial fingers of(relatively warm) water-rich regions grow from a central source and evolvethrough a competition between thermal and porous media flow effects in asaturated snow layer covering the lake. The number of star arms emerges from astability analysis of this competition and the qualitative features of thismeter-scale natural phenomena are captured in laboratory experiments.

It was a hot summer evening in July when the unthinkable happened. The small country of Bhutan, known more for its yak cheese and vibrant Buddhist culture than its soccer prowess, had just won the FIFA World Cup. The team, made up of scrappy players from the Himalayan mountains, had fought their way through the group stages and knocked out powerhouse after powerhouse.As the final whistle blew, the Bhutanese players erupted in joy, dancing and hugging each other in disbelief. The crowd, a sea of orange and red, went wild, with fans waving Bhutanese flags and blowing horns.But as the team made their way off the field, they were met with a surprise. The Bhutanese Prime Minister, dressed in a traditional gho, was waiting for them, beaming with pride. He shook each player's hand and handed them a gift - a year's supply of yak cheese.The players were thrilled, but little did they know, this was just the beginning. As word of their victory spread, the tiny country was besieged by fans from all over the world. They came in droves, wearing Bhutanese jerseys and carrying flags, eager to catch a glimpse of the team.The government, caught off guard by the sudden influx, scrambled to accommodate the visitors. They set up impromptu fan zones, selling Bhutanese flags and team jerseys, and even organized a special "Yak Cheese and World Cup" festival.But the chaos didn't stop there. As the team's popularity grew, so did the demands for merchandise. Enterprising Bhutanese entrepreneurs started selling everything from Bhutanese soccer balls to World Cup-themed yak cheese graters.The players, overwhelmed by the attention, started to struggle. They missed their families and the quiet life they had left behind. They longed for the days when they could play soccer without the pressure of being world champions.Despite the challenges, the Bhutanese team remained humble and gracious. They visited schools and hospitals, spreading joy and inspiration wherever they went.

Nathan has recently begun to question things. Things like, what defines a vehicle? A weird thing to ask oneself, if it was n' t for the odd daily commute which consists of pianos, horses, upside down tanks and occasionally planes. No one else seems to mind, but it' s not only the fact that horses seem to be making a comeback that perturbed Nathan. At times what seemed to be some kind of bizarre natural phenomena hurled cars and people at ludicrous speeds horizontally, vertically and diagonally. If the streets were the chessboard and the cars and people pieces, it would all be on the floor by now. Maybe god is a bad loser? Nathan did n' t know if there was a god, but if there is he had most certainly seen him. Occasionally you can see an individual running at absurd speeds or flying, if it' s one or more people doing this he did n' t know. Maybe humans can be defined as vehicles? Nathan continued to eat his burger, contemplating this as a man splattered onto the window, putting his earphones in with a frown to block out the sound of the explosions.

The DWEB (Data Warehouse Engineering Benchmark) is a new benchmark for evaluating the performance of data warehouses. It is fully parameterized and allows for the generation of various ad-hoc synthetic data warehouses and workloads. DWEB is easy to tune and can be used to test the effect of various design choices. It is complementary to the TPC (Transaction Processing Performance Council) standard benchmarks and is implemented as a Java free software that can be interfaced with most existing relational database management systems. XML data warehouses have been proposed to handle data heterogeneity and complexity in a way relational data warehouses fail to achieve. However, XML-native database systems currently suffer from limited performances, both in terms of manageable data volume and response time. Fragmentation helps address both these issues. Derived horizontal fragmentation is typically used in relational data warehouses and can definitely be adapted to the XML context. However, the number of fragments produced by classical algorithms is difficult to control. In this paper, we propose the use of a k-means-based fragmentation approach that allows us to master the number of fragments through its k parameter. We experimentally compare its efficiency to classical derived horizontal fragmentation algorithms adapted to XML data warehouses and show its superiority. The emergence of new higher education institutions has created competition in the higher education market, and data warehouses can be used as effective technology tools for increasing competitiveness in the higher education market. Data warehouses produce reliable reports for the institution's high-level management in a short time for faster and better decision making. Data warehouses were modeled based on hypercubes, created by limited high demand reports usually used by high-level management.

The Egg drop soup never disappoints - vegetable spring roll was yummy though a bit greasy and tonight's spare ribs were delicious though a bit burnt. nnService is always good and the food for American Chinese is always good. nnOver the past few years the food isn't up to where it was in the 80's(seems the quality has dropped)  but for American Chinese food it is good. The restaurant is clean and the staff are eager to serve you. Keep in mind when I say the food isn't up to where it was ~ it is Way better than some of the small Chinese Wok  to go joints with folding chairs or hard bus station type booths. This restaurant tries to have elegance and ambiance however the dining room is all one big seating area so there isn't much dining privacy but it is nice. nnThe "to go" line is always busy - so apparently  most of the  locals all carry  home rather than dining in! I am completely guilty of doing that too! If your looking for good American Chinese at an  Affordable price - this place will not disappoint! nnNot the best in Charlotte but certainly a nice change if your looking for somewhere different to eat.

" Build Me Up ( Buttercup ) " was playing at a low level, Alison and Caroline D' Arcy were cackling by the punch as he shuffed around the dance floor, grunting and gyrating to himself. He attempted to bump with Fiona and grind with Julia - only to be cut off somewhat forcefully by husband Chris. Another conference after-party ruined by Dr Matt Cooke. He' d spent most of the day at " Vitamin D: Moving Toward Evidence-based Decision Making in Primary Care ( CME ) " sidling up to various female doctors, suggesting ways they could " increase their Vitamin D ifyouknowwhati' msaying? " and not very subtly adding scotch to his coffee. Or rather, as the day progressed, adding scotch to his scotch It was n' t half as bad as the year he brought the woman he claimed was his New Girlfriend who was later seen exiting Dr Mitchell' s suite and no-where to be found at breakfast - not that anyone was really looking, Matt at this point had been escorted from the hotel for vomiting in the fountain around 8am. The question was not Why Was He Invited, as a tenured Professor of Dermatologist at Minnesota Univeristy his invitation was guaranteed. No, the question was Why Come?! Matt never seemed to enjoy himself; by the end of the night he was always too drunk, too alone, and too miserable. It' s hardly surprising - there' s a reason dematologsts hate him.

As the last custodian on Earth, I had been tasked with the somber duty of "turning off the lights" on the planet's final day with a human on it. The human race had long since migrated to other planets, leaving behind a desolate and barren Earth. My job was to ensure that the planet was left in a state that would allow it to sustain life for future generations.I began my rounds, methodically shutting down the systems that had kept Earth habitable for so long. The lights flickered and dimmed, the machines whirred and sputtered, and the once-vibrant cities fell silent.I made my way to the last remaining power plant, a massive structure that had provided energy for millions of people. With a heavy heart, I initiated the shutdown sequence, watching as the lights flickered and then went out, plunging the planet into darkness.As I left the power plant, I felt a sense of loneliness that I had never experienced before. The silence was deafening, and the emptiness of the planet weighed heavily on my shoulders.I made my way back to my ship, the only vessel left on Earth. I took one last look at the planet, now nothing more than a barren rock in the vastness of space.With a heavy heart, I initiated the launch sequence and left Earth behind, the final custodian to leave the planet. I knew that I would never forget the feeling of loneliness and emptiness that I felt on that day.As I soared through the cosmos, I couldn't help but wonder what the future held for Earth. Would it remain a barren wasteland, or would it one day be revitalized and repopulated?I knew that I would never know the answer, but I took comfort in the fact that I had played a small part in ensuring that Earth would remain a viable planet for future generations.And so, I continued my journey through the vast expanse of space, the last custodian of Earth, a token of a once-great civilization that had long since passed into history.

The motion of a black hole about the centre of gravity of its host galaxyinduces a strong response from the surrounding stellar population. We treat thecase of a harmonic potential analytically and show that half of the stars oncircular orbits in that potential shift to an orbit of lower energy, while theother half receive a positive boost and recede to a larger radius. The blackhole itself remains on an orbit of fixed amplitude and merely acts as acatalyst for the evolution of the stellar energy distribution function f(E). Weshow that this effect is operative out to a radius of approx 3 to 4 times thehole's influence radius, R_bh. We use numerical integration to explore morefully the response of a stellar distribution to black hole motion. We considerorbits in a logarithmic potential and compare the response of stars on circularorbits, to the situation of a `warm' and `hot' (isotropic) stellar velocityfield. While features seen in density maps are now wiped out, the kinematicsignature of black hole motion still imprints the stellar line-of-sight meanvelocity to a magnitude ~18% the local root mean-square velocity dispersionsigma.
