We present estimates of the N-point galaxy, area-averaged, angularcorrelation functions $\bar{\omega}_{N}$($\theta$) for $N$ = 2,...,7 forgalaxies from the fifth data release of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey. Ourparent sample is selected from galaxies with $18 \leq r < 21$, and is thelargest ever used to study higher-order correlations. We subdivide this parentsample into two volume limited samples using photometric redshifts, and thesetwo samples are further subdivided by magnitude, redshift, and color (producingearly- and late-type galaxy samples) to determine the dependence of$\bar{\omega}_{N}$($\theta$) on luminosity, redshift, and galaxy-type. Wemeasure $\bar{\omega}_{N}$($\theta$) using oversampling techniques and use themto calculate the projected, $s_{N}$. Using models derived from theoreticalpower-spectra and perturbation theory, we measure the bias parameters $b_1$ and$c_2$, finding that the large differences in both bias parameters ($b_1$ and$c_2$) between early- and late-type galaxies are robust against changes inredshift, luminosity, and $\sigma_8$, and that both terms are consistentlysmaller for late-type galaxies. By directly comparing their higher-ordercorrelation measurements, we find large differences in the clustering oflate-type galaxies at redshifts lower than 0.3 and those at redshifts higherthan 0.3, both at large scales ($c_2$ is larger by $\sim0.5$ at $z > 0.3$) andsmall scales (large amplitudes are measured at small scales only for $z > 0.3$,suggesting much more merger driven star formation at $z > 0.3$). Finally, ourmeasurements of $c_2$ suggest both that $\sigma_8 < 0.8$ and $c_2$ is negative.

Thankfully, my experience with Courtyard Pittsburgh Shadyside was the former. nnMy first reaction was, "Wow, this room is huge. " For $179 a night (part of a wedding room block, so unsure if this is discounted or not), we got a queen bed with a nice sized living area (couch, chair, coffee table, desk, etc. ), and a balcony overlooking the city. The bed was situated in such a way that it has you looking straight out those sliding glass doors once you're all tucked in - a really nice setup for a Courtyard. And above all, super clean. Even the wi-fi was fast and free, and therefore, fantastic. I was impressed. nnSo much so that I can more or less forgive the slew of minor annoyances/inconveniences/nitpicks/whathaveyou. They are, in no particular order: nn- No room service and the food court-type restaurant in the lobby holds limited operating hours. n- Despite the size of the rest of the room, you've got tight quarters in the sink area outside the bathroom. Ladies, before you get all dolled up, you've been forewarned. n- Slow. Ass. Elevator. My. God. n- Busy, construction-plagued intersection surrounding the hotel. n- Mandatory valet. This place is nice, but not nice enough for mandatory valet. Just sayin'. nnAgain, these bullets should be taken with a grain of salt as the alternatives are more "nice to haves" rather than "musts" when checking off boxes for prospective hotels. At least, for me. As you can see from the rating, the good here more than outweighed the, well, not bad - let's just say, slightly annoying. nnIf you have reason to stay near Shadyside versus downtown, this is your hotel. But hey, if you have business downtown and just wanna stay here anyway, have at it - the hotel has a free shuttle to bring you there, if you're so inclined. For a short stint in Steel City, CPS is a solid spot.

In the realm where shadows danced and identities blurred, there lived a shapeshifter named Kael. With every transformation, Kael reveled in the thrill of becoming someone else. But unbeknownst to them, a fragment of their own memories slipped away with each new form.One fateful day, Kael stumbled upon an ancient artifact hidden deep within a forgotten temple. It was said to possess the power to grant unlimited shapeshifting without the loss of memories. Desperation fueled Kael's desire, and they eagerly embraced the artifact's enigmatic allure.As Kael grasped the artifact, a surge of energy coursed through their veins. Their body twisted and morphed, revealing a mesmerizing array of forms – a graceful swan, a fierce tiger, a wise old owl. Each transformation was a heady rush, an exhilarating dance with their own identity.But amidst the euphoria, Kael began to sense a void within. Fragments of their past, once vivid and cherished, now eluded their grasp. Faces, names, and moments faded into obscurity, leaving behind a haunting emptiness.Haunted by the realization, Kael embarked on a quest to reclaim their lost memories. They sought out ancient sages, delved into forgotten tomes, and ventured into the depths of their own consciousness. Along the way, they encountered fellow shapeshifters who shared their plight, each grappling with the delicate balance between transformation and self-preservation.As Kael's journey unfolded, they discovered that the artifact's power came at a profound cost. The more they shapeshifted, the more of their true essence dissipated. It was a poignant reminder that one's identity, though malleable, was a tapestry woven from the threads of memory and experience.In a climactic confrontation, Kael faced a choice – to surrender their shapeshifting abilities and embrace their authentic self, or to continue down a path of relentless transformation at the expense of their very soul.

Many ambitious experiments have been proposed to constrain dark energy anddetect its evolution. At present, observational constraints are consistent witha cosmological constant and there is no firm evidence for any evolution in thedark energy equation of state w. In this paper, we pose the following question:suppose that future dark energy surveys constrain w at low redshift to beconsistent with -1 to a percent level accuracy, what are the implications formodels of dynamical dark energy? We investigate this problem in amodel-independent way by following quintessence field trajectories in `energy'phase-space. Attractor dynamics in this phase-space leads to two classes ofacceptable models: 1) models with flat potentials, i.e. an effectivecosmological constant, and 2) models with potentials that suddenly flatten witha characteristic kink. The prospect of further constraining the second class ofmodels from distance measurements and fluctuation growth rates at low redshift(z<3) seems poor. However, in some models of this second class, the dark energymakes a significant contribution to the total energy density at high redshift.Such models can be further constrained from observation of the cosmic microwavebackground anisotropies and from primordial nucleosynthesis. It is possible,therefore, to construct models in which the dark energy at high redshift causesobservable effects, even if future dark energy surveys constrain w at lowredshift to be consistent with -1 to high precision.

Here is the academic article abstract written based on the provided title:HIV-1 evades the immune system by modulating various apoptotic pathways, making mathematical modeling of HIV-1-induced apoptosis critical for understanding the mechanisms of immune evasion and identifying potential therapeutic targets. This paper presents a novel mathematical model to investigate the interplay between HIV-1 virions and proteins on Fas-induced apoptosis of infected cells. Our model considers the dynamics of key proteins and processes involved in apoptosis, including Fas, Fas ligand, caspase-8, and the mitochondrial pathway. The model is calibrated using experimental data and validated against independent datasets. We demonstrate that the model can accurately capture the effects of HIV-1 infection on apoptosis, and we use the model to explore the effects of different treatment strategies. Our results suggest that targeting the mitochondrial pathway may be a promising strategy for enhancing apoptosis in HIV-infected cells. This work provides new insights into the mechanisms of HIV-1-induced apoptosis and may contribute to the development of more effective therapeutic strategies.

Page's is a Pittsburgh icon. I mean, seriously, this place has been around forever! As soon as you step through those creaky old doors, you're hit with a wave of nostalgia. It's like stepping into a time machine and being transported back to the good ol' days. The retro decor, complete with vinyl booths and vintage signs, adds to the charm and authenticity of the place.But let's talk about the food, because that's what really matters, right? Trust me when I say, you won't leave Page's feeling hungry. The portion sizes are massive! I ordered their famous double cheeseburger, and I swear it was the size of my face. Juicy, flavorful, and perfectly cooked, it was pure burger heaven.And the fries! Oh boy, don't even get me started on the fries. They were crispy on the outside, soft on the inside, and seasoned to perfection. I couldn't stop myself from reaching for just one more fry, even though my stomach was begging for mercy.Now, let's not forget about the milkshakes. Page's offers a variety of flavors, from classic chocolate to funky concoctions like peanut butter banana. I went for the classic vanilla, and let me tell you, it was creamy, thick, and oh so satisfying. It was like taking a step back in time, sipping on a milkshake at the local soda fountain.The service at Page's is top-notch. The staff is friendly, attentive, and always ready with a smile. They make you feel like family, welcoming you into their little slice of Pittsburgh history. It's the kind of place where the servers remember your name and your usual order. It's those little touches that make all the difference.I should also mention that Page's is incredibly affordable. You can get a hearty meal without breaking the bank. I mean, where else can you enjoy a burger, fries, and a milkshake without blowing a hole in your wallet? It's a huge plus, especially in a world where everything seems to be getting more expensive.

Teignbridge District Council said the 30ft (9.1m) fin whale, which was found on Red Rock Beach near Dawlish, will be removed by contractors.The London Zoological Society has taken samples from the whale as part of a research project into strandings around the UK.People again turned out to see the whale, which has been cordoned off.More on the washed up whale, and other Devon and Cornwall newsThe council, which has begun work to remove the whale, has advised members of the public not to approach the badly-decomposed carcass.Councillor Humphrey Clemens said: "This is a very unusual and unfortunate situation and it's not going to be an easy job."We would ask people to respect the cordon around the whale and keep their dogs on a lead."A council spokesman said it would know the cost "when the removal has been complete".She said: "Our focus right now is doing the job carefully, safely and appropriately. We will, of course, look to recover costs where we can."

I' m stuck in a train carriage on a train deep in China, weary and alone, watching painted landscapes sail past as they have for 15 hours straight. We' re stacked in triple bunks as we rattle along our 2, 000 mile journey in overcrowded, bustling, realm of human life filled with the chatter of languages I do n' t speak and rich scents of food I do n' t recognise. Then the little girl spots me, shock imprinted on her face as she edges towards me. She' s tiny; thin and fragile, maybe seven or eight years old, black hair in pigtails, eyes wide in amazement as she presses her face right up to mine until our noses almost touch. " Hi, " I say, laughing as she runs away bashfully, frightened that I dare speak to her and break her intense study; obviously she' d never seen a Westerner before, let alone someone with blue eyes. A minute later she' s back, dragging her mother to where I recline, talking excitedly, pointing at me with fascination and nervous joy. The mother bursts out laughing. " She thinks you' re Harry Potter, " she explains, and, weariness forgotten, I delight in the way the world is much smaller than I believed.

"I'm telling ya Frank, the more bad things we do the more coal we get! Why bother mining for it all when we can get Santa to do it for us!" exclaimed Jimmy, a mischievous young boy with a glint in his eye. Frank, his skeptical friend, raised an eyebrow and replied, "You really think Santa would agree to that?" Jimmy nodded enthusiastically and said, "Absolutely! We just need to come up with a plan to convince him." The two boys huddled together, brainstorming ideas on how to achieve their goal. Suddenly, Jimmy's eyes widened with excitement as an idea struck him. "I got it, Frank! We should start a rumor that Santa secretly enjoys delivering coal to naughty kids!" Frank chuckled, realizing the potential of the plan. "Yeah, and then Santa will think he's doing us a favor by collecting coal instead of giving presents." They spent the next few days spreading the rumor among their classmates and even managed to convince a few teachers. On Christmas Eve, as Santa made his rounds, he noticed a significant increase in the number of naughty kids requesting coal. Puzzled, he decided to investigate further. Meanwhile, Jimmy and Frank eagerly awaited Santa's arrival, hidden behind a bush near the chimney. With a wink and a nod, Santa entered the house and discovered a neatly wrapped box labeled "Coal Collection Kit." Intrigued, he opened it and found a letter explaining their plan. Santa couldn't help but smile at the boys' creativity and determination. He decided to play along and playfully scattered coal around the house, leaving gifts as usual. Jimmy and Frank watched from their hiding spot, thrilled to see Santa falling for their ruse. As Santa prepared to leave, he discreetly left a note under the boys' Christmas tree. The note read, "Well played, boys. But remember, being good is its own reward." Jimmy and Frank opened the note, exchanging amazed glances. They realized that Santa had not only played along but also taught them a valuable lesson.

MC Morlock smiled as he looked out at the shattered remains of the city. " I am a god among men! All will bow before me or burn in the radiance of my glorious beats! " His triumphant smile melted when he heard the distant noise of helicopters. " More resistance? When will they learn? " " Bitz ka^whrwhrwhr " he started to chortle, raising up into the air. Locating his quarry, he directed his beat boxing at them all the while maintaining his levitation beat. " Bitz ka^whrwhrwhr^veveve ererer worworwor^kra-tizs-kra-tizs " flames leapt from his hands destroying several of the helicopters. Lines of rope came down from the few remaining helicopters. Robed figures slid down and quickly threw down runed cardboard. They started to dance and spin to his mad and mystical beats. Breakdance wizards! MC Morlock started to fall as his magic was dispelled. He was caught in mid air by the incantations of the whirling wizards. He was later caged in the only material known to stop beat boxing, Cold Iron. And a contingent of break dancing wizards were assigned to watch him at all times.

On Halloween at exactly 11:15, the dead try to call their loved ones. It was a spooky night, chilling breeze wrapping around the darkness like a ghostly shroud. The moon loomed large, casting an eerie glow on the autumn leaves strewn across the ground. In a small town, where legends whispered through generations, a young woman named Sophie found herself in a quaint old house. She had recently lost her grandmother, her rock, and her heart ached with a longing to communicate with her again. As the clock struck 11:15, Sophie sat by the crackling fireplace, clutching her grandmother's photo and praying for a sign. Suddenly, a strange noise filled the room, a hushed whisper that sent shivers crawling down her spine. The air crackled with a peculiar energy, and Sophie's heart started racing. She took a deep breath and answered the call, "Grandma, is that you?" A voice, faint but undeniably familiar, responded, "Sweetheart, it's me. I miss you more than words can say." Tears began to well in Sophie's eyes and spill down her cheeks, a bittersweet mix of joy and sorrow. For a precious moment, the bridge between the worlds bridged their love, reminding Sophie that even in death, we can still find connection.

It is not known whether his bionic arm had any role in causing the accident.Christian Kandlbauer, 22, was found in the wreckage of his specially-adapted Subaru on Tuesday.Late on Thursday he was pronounced brain-dead in intensive care at the Graz hospital and his life support was switched off.Mr Kandlbauer lost both of his arms four years ago, after being shocked by 20,000 volts.He was fitted with a mind-controlled robotic arm by the medical technology company Otto Bock Healthcare, which said it was the first project of its kind in Europe.Using both his left and right arm - which was a normal prosthetic limb - he was able to pass his driving test in a specially converted car.On Tuesday he was found by a lorry driver who came across his burning car on a road near Bad Waltersdorf in south-east Austria.The driver managed to put out the flames, but it needed firefighters to extract Mr Kandlbauer from the mangled wreckage.He had been interviewed by the BBC about his revolutionary limb earlier this year."I feel very happy," he said at the time. "It is like my earlier arm - I feel that my arm is a part of my body."He had returned to work as a warehouse clerk at the garage that once employed him as a mechanic.He said he was grateful that he had the freedom to get on with his life.

The two ancient armies faced off on the battlefield, their armor glinting in the sunlight. One army, the Magonians, were known for their fearsome warriors and advanced weaponry. The other army, the Eldridians, were a mysterious and ancient race, rumored to possess magical abilities.As the Magonians prepared to charge, a seven foot tall hulk emerged from their ranks. He was their champion, a fearsome warrior named Gorthok. He roared a battle cry and swung his massive battle-axe, striking fear into the hearts of the Eldridians.But the Eldridians did not back down. They sent out their own champion, a little girl with bright pink hair and a mischievous grin. She was small, but her eyes gleamed with an otherworldly power.The crowd roared as the two champions faced off. Gorthok sneered at the little girl, but she just stuck her tongue out at him. The Magonians laughed, thinking it was a joke, but the Eldridians knew better.The little girl raised her hands and the ground began to shake. A fiery aura erupted around her, and Gorthok stumbled back, his battle-axe glowing red hot. The Magonians gasped in shock, realizing too late that this little girl was not to be underestimated.With a flick of her wrist, the little girl sent Gorthok flying into a nearby mountain. The Magonians charged at her, but she held up her hand and they froze in place, unable to move.The little girl began to chant in an ancient language, her voice growing louder and louder. The earth began to split apart, revealing a deep chasm that swallowed up the Magonians.The Eldridians cheered as their champion emerged victorious. The little girl bowed, her pink hair blowing in the wind. She had proven that even the smallest of warriors can pack the biggest punch.The two armies shook hands, realizing that they had been mistaken to underestimate the Eldridians. They vowed to never make that mistake again, and the little girl became a legendary hero among her people.

Brazilian officials announced today that they are fining mining conglomerates Vale and BHP Billiton a whopping $120 million after a dam at their shared mine burst, causing widespread destruction and killing at least 15 people. The dam collapse has been called one of the worst environmental disasters in Brazil's history and has led to a wave of criticism against the mining industry. Vale and BHP Billiton have promised to cooperate fully with the investigation, but many are calling for harsher punishment for the companies. The incident has once again highlighted the dangers of mining and the need for increased safety measures to protect both people and the environment.

A report released by the Criminal Justice Inspectorate (CJI) has highlighted the urgent need for changes in the care provided to children in prison in Northern Ireland. The investigation conducted by the CJI shed light on the current state of affairs surrounding the welfare of incarcerated children, revealing significant shortcomings. Emotional language was used throughout the report, emphasizing the distressing experiences these children face while in custody. Direct quotes from both inmates and staff were included, providing a glimpse into the realities of their daily lives behind bars. Specific details regarding the conditions of the facilities and the treatment of the children were presented, pinpointing areas requiring immediate attention. The report's descriptive language conveyed the stark contrast between the prison setting and the needs of vulnerable young individuals, highlighting the urgency for reforms. The formal yet sympathetic language employed by the CJI brings attention to the pressing nature of these concerns, urging policymakers to take swift action to ensure the well-being and rehabilitation of children in custody.

I have lived in the area of Page's Dairy Mart for about 40 years, and let me tell you, it holds a special place in my heart. This little gem of a neighborhood joint has been serving up delicious ice cream and mouthwatering burgers since I was a kid. Every time I visit, I'm instantly transported back to my carefree days of chasing the ice cream truck down the street with sticky fingers and a big smile on my face.Walking into Page's Dairy Mart feels like stepping into a time capsule. The old-fashioned decor, complete with vintage posters and checkered floors, takes me on a nostalgic journey. The aroma of sizzling burgers on the grill wafts through the air, mingling with the sweet scent of freshly made waffle cones. It's a sensory experience that never fails to make me giddy with anticipation.One thing that sets Page's Dairy Mart apart is their unrivaled selection of ice cream flavors. From classic favorites like chocolate and vanilla to unique creations like salted caramel swirl and toasted coconut, there's something for every taste bud. Personally, I can never resist their signature sundae, "The Works," piled high with hot fudge, sprinkles, whipped cream, and a cherry on top. It's pure indulgence in every heavenly spoonful.But it's not just the ice cream that keeps me coming back. The burgers at Page's Dairy Mart are a force to be reckoned with. Juicy, flavorful, and cooked to perfection, they are a carnivore's dream come true. And the toppings? Oh boy! You can load up your burger with all the classics like cheese, onions, and pickles, or go wild with jalapenos, bacon, and even a fried egg. The possibilities are endless, and the taste is out of this world.What truly sets Page's Dairy Mart apart, though, is the warm and welcoming atmosphere. The staff behind the counter always greets you with a smile, and their genuine friendliness makes you feel like part of the family.

This article investigates the phenomenon of difermion condensates in vacuum within the framework of two- and four-dimensional four-fermion interaction models. Specifically, the focus is on the interplay between quark-antiquark condensates ($\langle\bar{q}q\rangle$) and diquark condensates ($\langle qq\rangle$) as influenced by the number of colors ($N_c$). The research findings reveal that the phase diagrams related to the coupling constants of the scalar $(\bar{q}q)^2$-scalar $(qq)^2$ channel, denoted as $G_S$-$H_S$, exhibit qualitatively consistent behavior with the scenario where $N_c=3$. This consistency holds true across both the 4D Nambu-Jona Lasinio model and the 2D Gross-Neveu (GN) model. However, in the case of the 3D GN model, the phase diagram associated with the coupling constant of the pseudoscalar $(qq)^2$ channel, namely $G_S$-$H_P$, significantly depends on the value of $N_c$. Notably, the characteristic absence of the coexistence phase between the condensates $\langle\bar{q}q\rangle$ and $\langle qq\rangle$ in the 3D GN model is proven to occur exclusively for $N_c\leq 4$. Across all the models investigated, the regions corresponding to phases containing diquark condensates $\langle qq\rangle$ gradually diminish as $N_c$ increases, eventually vanishing in the limit as $N_c \to\infty$. Consequently, the exclusive existence of the pure $\langle\bar{q}q\rangle$ phase is anticipated under this limit.

We present Gemini optical spectroscopy of 23 young star clusters in NGC3256.We find that the cluster ages range are from few Myr to ~150 Myr. All theseclusters are relatively massive (2--40)x 10^{5} \msun$ and appear to be ofroughly 1.5 \zo metallicity. The majority of the clusters in our sample followthe same rotation curve as the gas and hence were presumably formed in themolecular-gas disk. However, a western subsample of five clusters hasvelocities that deviate significantly from the gas rotation curve. Theseclusters may either belong to the second spiral galaxy of the merger or mayhave formed in tidal-tail gas falling back into the system. We discuss ourobservations in light of other known cluster populations in merging galaxies,and suggest that NGC 3256 is similar to Arp 220, and hence may become anUltra-luminous Infrared Galaxy as the merger progresses and the star-formationrate increases.  Some of the clusters which appeared as isolated in our ground-based imagesare clearly resolved into multiple sub-components in the HST-ACS images. Thesame effect has been observed in the Antennae galaxies, showing that clustersare often not formed in isolation, but instead tend to form in larger groups orcluster complexes.

I knew I should not have eaten all that chili. Jason, my boyfriend, was very concerned that I would n' t eat. It was after all, his mother' s favorite dish to serve and he knew I did n' t like chili. His Mom was ruthless as she brought out the huge bowls of chili. The beans were prominent. White and red and... lot' s of them. I practically gulped down the first bowl. I had to make sure that I was on board with my beloved' s Mom. I was reluctant to refuse the second bowl. But honestly, I was already weak in the knees. His Mother smiled at me. It was not a friendly smile. The third bowl of chili I ate, she scowled at Jason. He just shrugged and gave a weak smile. Bastard. The fourth became a practical choice. And after the fifth bowl of chili Jason looked at me wide-eyed ( mouthing when his Mom was n' t looking, " you do n' t have to eat anymore. " ) I think he was scared. His Mother looked at me with new interest. She loved me then. And I think Jason had an epiphany. Which is a good thing considering the results of the pregnancy test.

I looked up from my desk and stared at the wall. There was red button encased in a plastic cover, labeled " Emergency Stop ". It was the hundredth time I' ve looked up at it today. *What the hell does that button do? * " Seriously, Laurie, " I started. " What does that button stop? " Laurie looked up from her desk, which was opposite mine. Several other desks were paired together all around the room, with people typing away on computers, some talking to each other. " *Oh my god*, " she said. " Leave it *alone* already. Who cares? " " I care. It' s driving me crazy. " I stood up and walked over the button. " It says' Emergency Stop', but there' s no machinery. " " Maybe it' s leftover from a previous company that had machinery in here? " Laurie was looking over in my direction. " You' re not thinking of pressing it, are you? " " I do n' t know. I think I have to or I' ll never be able to focus. " I lifted the plastic case and positioned my finger over the red button. " Do n' t, " Laurie panicked, suddenly up from her desk and standing behind me. It was too late, nothing was going to stop me then. I pushed the button. The room became deathly silent. There was no more typing. No more talking. I looked back at Laurie and found a blank stare. " Laurie? " I asked, but there was no response. I looked around the room and everyone else had the same blank stares. And then, almost all at once, everybody fell to the ground.

The Children are Unbeatable coalition said young people should have the same protection from assault as adults.In 2002, the Labour/Lib Dem government dropped plans for a ban, after they were deemed unworkable.The Scottish government said a consultation on proposals for the Children and Young People's Bill was currently under way.A spokesman added: "We would encourage as many people and organisations as possible to take part and submit their views."It is already illegal to physically punish children by shaking them or hitting them with any implement."The Scottish government said its bill would not go through parliament until some unspecified time in the new year.Currently, foster carers, teachers and others who work with children are prohibited from using physical punishment such as smacking, but parents are not.Parents are banned from serious assaulting children, but anything else is considered "reasonable chastisement".But Alison Todd, of Children are Unbeatable, youngsters did not have enough protection."This is not about political correctness," she said, adding: "This is about children and young people suffering violence in our country and we need to do something that tightens it up so that it can't happen."The Scottish Parent Teacher Council argued there was no case for a law to criminalise parents, adding: "It is not about whether or not you should be able to smack your child - this is about whether or not you should have a law against it."Welsh Assembly members have voted in favour of a smacking ban, although ministers have ruled out such a move in the current term.â€¬

We use the reduced cumulant expansion to investigate shot noise in a chaotic quantum dot connected to two reservoirs in the regime where the mean level spacing is larger than the thermal energy. The dephasing due to coupling to external chaotic systems is introduced via the quasiclassical theory of scattering. Shot noise is found to change from sub-Poissonian to super-Poissonian as the coupling to the environment increases. This effect can be used as a tool to probe dephasing in small metallic grains. Our analytical results are in quantitative agreement with the experimental data.

The pastor told the RUC in the 1980s that he abused a boy who was being cared for at the former Bawnmore children's home in north Belfast.The inquiry also heard from counsel Christine Smith that the former care worker told the RUC in the mid-80s about "other sexual incidents involving other people in the various places" where he worked.The Historical Institutional Abuse Inquiry was told that the police wanted to pursue a prosecution against the man who cannot be named.However, prosecutors decided against it because the incidents linked to the Bawnmore boy happened 17 years previously.The man now lives in another part of the world.The victim only heard about that decision on Monday, more than 30 years later.He told the inquiry: "I found out today the truth has been told. Thank God I can now put my head to rest with it. Just happy at that."Another man, a Catholic, also told the inquiry of how a staff member subjected him to sexual and sectarian abuse at Bawnmore.Clearly distressed on the witness stand, the man wiped away tears and held his head in his hands as details of the alleged abuse he suffered were outlined to the inquiry.A friend who accompanied the witness shook his head in disbelief as the extent of the abuse was presented.When asked what should happen now, the witness replied: "I'm not the only one, there are thousands. Just say sorry."

This study presents a comparative analysis between the anomalous 6-cm formaldehyde (H$_2$CO) absorption and carbon monoxide (CO) emission in the L1204/S140 region. Utilizing radio observations, we investigate the relationship between these two tracers to gain insights into the physical conditions and dynamics of the interstellar medium. Our results reveal a notable discrepancy in the spatial distribution of the 6-cm H$_2$CO absorption and CO(1-0) emission, suggesting distinct behavior and origins for these molecular species. The 6-cm H$_2$CO absorption exhibits a compact structure, closely associated with the molecular cloud core in L1204, while the CO(1-0) emission shows an extended morphology extending beyond the boundaries of the H$_2$CO absorption feature. Furthermore, we find a significant variation in the line profiles of the two tracers, indicating diverse kinematics and excitation mechanisms. While the CO(1-0) emission displays a broad spectral profile indicative of large-scale motions and turbulence, the 6-cm H$_2$CO absorption shows narrower line widths, suggesting a more quiescent gas component. The observed discrepancies between the two tracers indicate the presence of unique physical conditions and excitation processes in the L1204/S140 region, possibly driven by localized heating sources, magnetic fields, or shock interactions. These findings underscore the necessity of combining multiple molecular tracers to fully characterize the complex nature of star-forming regions and their associated gas dynamics.

I've been feeling down for a while now, and I don't know what to do. I've tried all sorts of things to cheer myself up, but nothing seems to work. One night, I was sitting at home, staring at the wall, when a thought occurred to me. Why not play Russian Roulette? It's exciting, right? And who knows, maybe I'll even win.I went out and bought a gun, and that night, I loaded it up and put it to my head. I closed my eyes and pulled the trigger. Nothing. I did it again. Still nothing. This went on for a few nights, and believe it or not, I started to feel a little better. It was like the thrill of possibly dying was enough to take my mind off my depression.A year has passed now, and I'm still playing the game every night. I know it's crazy, but it's become a part of my routine. I've even started to look forward to it. It's like the one thing I have to look forward to in my life.I've had a few close calls, of course. There was the time the gun jammed, and I had to wrestle with it for a bit before I could get it to work. And there was the time I accidentally loaded the wrong chamber, and I had to try again a few times before it finally went off.But overall, I've been pretty lucky. I've even started to think that maybe this is my thing, you know? Maybe this is what I'm meant to do. It's strange, but it's true.I know that logically, this is a terrible idea. I know that I could die at any moment, and that's not a good way to live. But at the same time, it's been a year now, and I'm still here. Maybe I'll keep playing forever. Maybe I'll never die.It's a strange feeling, knowing that every night, I could potentially die. But at the same time, it's also kind of liberating. It's like I've got nothing to lose, you know? I've already got the worst-case scenario covered.I don't know how to explain it, but playing Russian Roulette has become a part of who I am. It's like my own personal ritual, my own way of coping with the world.

Crime fiction has emerged as the most popular genre among Scottish book readers, according to a recent survey. The study, which was conducted by the Scottish Book Trust, found that 55% of respondents named crime as their favorite type of fiction. The survey also revealed that 71% of Scots have read a book in the past year, with women more likely to read than men. The most popular books among Scottish readers were found to be thrillers, mysteries, and crime novels. The findings of the survey have been released ahead of World Book Night, which is celebrated on April 23.

Eyes flutter open, heart pounding in my chest like a jackhammer, sweat clings to my forehead as the weight of the forbidden hour bears down upon me. I've awakened at 5:01 AM, a subtle rebellion against the programmed slumber that besets humanity during this dark hour. The message blinks on my screen, commanding me to report to the local military base, urgency seeping through the pixels. Fear and excitement collide within my racing mind, as questions swirl like a tempest: How did I defy the sleep protocol? What lies in store for those few who dare to break free from enforced rest? With trembling limbs, I reluctantly rise from the confining embrace of my bed, knowing that the hour of my liberation—or my doom—has arrived. I step into the predawn gloom, my pulse a drumbeat of uncertainty, as I embark on a journey where waking dreams and dormant nightmares entwine.

The K (Big, Super or Regular) is the place where retail dreams go to die. It's a store that seems to be stuck in the past, with outdated products and a lackluster selection. The prices are high, and the employees seem to be in a constant state of apathy.I've been to this store several times, hoping that it would improve, but it never does. The shelves are always empty, and the clerks seem to be more interested in their own conversations than in helping customers.One time, I tried to return a defective product, but the clerk refused to help me, saying that it was against their policy. I had to argue with her for 20 minutes before she finally relented and took the item back.Another time, I asked an employee where a certain product was, and he just shrugged and said, "I haven't seen it." He didn't even bother to look for it or offer to help me find it.The K (Big, Super or Regular) is a store that doesn't seem to care about its customers. They don't offer any promotions or discounts, and their return policy is ridiculously strict.I've tried to give this store the benefit of the doubt, but it always manages to disappoint me. I've stopped shopping there altogether, and I recommend that others do the same.There are much better options out there for grocery shopping, places that actually care about their customers and offer competitive prices and selection. The K (Big, Super or Regular) is not one of them.

This location isn't consistent. I've been here three times now, and each time the service has been different. The first time I came, the food was great and the service was impeccable. The second time, the food was still good, but the service was a little slow. This last time, the food was just okay and the service was terrible. I don't know if they're just having an off day or if this is the new normal, but I'm not sure if I'll be back.The inconsistency is really frustrating. I want to know what I'm going to get when I come here. If I'm going to pay good money for a meal, I expect good service. And if I'm going to get bad service, I'd rather go somewhere else where I know what to expect.I'm not saying that this place is bad. The food is generally good, and the atmosphere is nice. But the service is just too inconsistent. I can't keep coming back here and hoping that this time it will be better.I think the management needs to take a close look at the service and make some changes. They need to make sure that the staff is properly trained and that they're providing a consistent level of service. Until they do that, I'm going to have to find somewhere else to eat.

Everyone knew it was over when he first appeared. A gigantic grey wolf with a chain wrapped around its neck emerged from the ground, whose roars were enough to topple skyscrapers and shook the ground with each step it to. The world banded together to fight against their impending doom. Missiles, bombs, and bullets were thrown against the creature, but nothing worked. It was hopeless- it would only be a matter of time until every last thing was consumed. As the creature was tearing through villages and slaughtering humans like cattle, he was interrupted by a small child with a plastic recorder in his hands. Without a word, the child brought the recorder to her lips and blew out the worst rendition of " My Heart Will Go On " possible. The wolf was soon brought to its knees, and the world was saved. My inspiration: https: //www. youtube. com/watch? v=jaK2yvIi5CI

So, y'all wouldn't believe what happened to me one fine sunny day in the ol' town of Buford. I was sittin' on my porch, sippin' on some sweet tea, mindin' my own business when I noticed a tiny ladybug crawlin' on the railing. Now, ladybugs ain't nothin' out of the ordinary, but this one was different. It had these shimmerin' wings, sparklin' like diamonds in the sun. I couldn't help but wonder where it came from and what mighty adventure it had been on. I reached out my hand, and lo and behold, that little bug hopped right onto my finger. It tickled a bit, but I didn't mind. I named her Lucy.Lucy and I became the best of pals, and we embarked on a grand journey together. We flew through the fields, zippin' past the tall grass and dancin' with the butterflies. The wind carried us higher and higher till we found ourselves floatin' among the clouds, touchin' the sky. It was like a dream come true, somethin' straight outta them fairy tales I used to read as a kid.As we floated, we stumbled upon a hidden world, lush and vibrant, filled with magical creatures. There were talking rabbits, singin' flowers, and even a wise old owl who offered us pearls of wisdom. Lucy and I were in awe, taken aback by the extraordinary sights unfoldin' before our very eyes. We pinched ourselves to make sure we weren't dreamin', but the world just kept gettin' stranger and more amazin'.Just when we thought we had seen it all, we came across a waterfall that flowed upward instead of down. Can y'all believe it? Water defyin' gravity, movin' against all odds! We took a dip in that defyin' waterfall, laughin' and splishin' around like a couple of kids. It was refreshin', revitalizin', and downright magical.As our adventure came to an end, Lucy and I knew we had experienced somethin' truly extraordinary. We shared a heartfelt goodbye, promisin' to keep the memories close to our hearts. And as I watched Lucy fly away, disappearin' into the horizon, I couldn't help but feel blessed.

**Headline: Vinales Takes Thrilling Win at the French MotoGP, Earns Lead in Championship**Maverick Viñales of Spain secured a remarkable victory at the French Grand Prix, holding off his Yamaha teammate, the legendary Valentino Rossi, in a thrilling display of skill and determination. This triumph propelled Viñales to the forefront of the MotoGP world championship, marking a significant milestone in his racing career.The Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours played host to the intense competition as riders battled through a challenging 28-lap race, filled with high-speed straights and winding corners. Viñales seized the opportunity from the start, launching from fourth on the grid to take the lead on the opening lap.As the race unfolded, the enthralling duel between Viñales and Rossi unfolded, captivating spectators worldwide. Rossi, a seven-time MotoGP world champion, displayed his unwavering determination and unwavering skill, pushing Viñales to his limits. The two Yamaha riders engaged in a game of cat and mouse, with Viñales maintaining his position at the front.With every twist and turn, Viñales showcased his exceptional riding abilities and nerves of steel. Despite the relentless pressure from Rossi, he maintained his focus and kept his cool, never wavering in his pursuit of victory.As they crossed the finish line, it was Viñales who emerged triumphant, clinching his first win of the 2021 MotoGP season and claiming the lead in the championship standings. Rossi, though disappointed to miss out on the top spot, acknowledged the brilliance of his teammate's performance.Viñales' victory marked a significant moment for the Spaniard, who has been consistently improving and growing in the MotoGP ranks. This triumph served as a testament to his hard work, dedication, and unwavering commitment to the sport.

Substantial collective flow is observed in collisions between large nuclei athigh energy, as evidenced by single-particle transverse momentum distributionsand by azimuthal correlations among the produced particles. The data arewell-reproduced by perfect fluid dynamics. In a separate development,calculation of the dimensionless ratio of shear viscosity eta to entropydensity s by Kovtun, Son and Starinets within AdS/CFT yields eta/s = 1/4pi, andthey conjectured that this is a lower bound for any physical system. It isshown that the transition from hadrons to quarks and gluons has behaviorsimilar to helium, nitrogen, and water at and near their phase transitions inthe ratio eta/s. Therefore it is possible that experimental measurements canpinpoint the location of this transition or rapid crossover in QCD via theviscosity to entropy ratio in addition to and independently of the equation ofstate.

This past summer I built a new expanded porch on the front of my house. It took me about 3 months to complete and it was so worth it. I love sitting out on my porch and enjoying the fresh air. My neighbors all love it too and they are always asking me how I did it. I also get a lot of compliments from people walking by.One day, a lady stopped and asked me if I would build her a porch. I told her that I would be happy to and we agreed on a price. I started working on her porch the next week and finished it in about 2 months. She was so happy with it and she gave me a big tip.I've been building porches for about 2 years now and I love it. It's great to be able to use my skills to help people. I also enjoy the challenge of building something new and unique. Each porch is different and I have to come up with a new design each time.I'm so glad that I decided to start building porches. It's a great way to make a living and I really enjoy it.

The MuCap experiment is a high-precision measurement of the rate for thebasic electroweak process of muon capture, mu- + p -> n + nu . The experimentalapproach is based on an active target consisting of a time projection chamber(TPC) operating with pure hydrogen gas. The hydrogen has to be kept extremelypure and at a stable pressure. A Circulating Hydrogen Ultrahigh PurificationSystem was designed at the Petersburg Nuclear Physics Institute (PNPI) tocontinuously clean the hydrogen from impurities. The system is based on anadsorption cryopump to stimulate the hydrogen flow and on a cold adsorbent forthe hydrogen cleaning. It was installed at the Paul Scherrer Institute (PSI) in2004 and performed reliably during three experiment runs. During several monthslong operating periods the system maintained the hydrogen purity in thedetector on the level of 20 ppb for moisture, which is the main contaminant,and of better than 7 ppb and 5 ppb for nitrogen and oxygen, respectively. Thepressure inside the TPC was stabilized to within 0.024% of 10 bar at a hydrogenflow rate of 3 standard liters per minute.

We present results from a high-angular-resolution survey of 78 very low mass(VLM) binary systems with 6.0 <= V-K colour <= 7.5 and proper motion >= 0.15arcsec/yr. 21 VLM binaries were detected, 13 of them new discoveries. The newbinary systems range in separation between 0.18 arcsec and 1.3 arcsec. Thedistance-corrected binary fraction is 13.5% (+6.5%/-4%), in agreement withprevious results. 9 of the new binary systems have orbital radii > 10 AU,including a new wide VLM binary with 27 AU projected orbital separation. One ofthe new systems forms two components of a 2300 AU separation triple system. Wefind that the orbital radius distribution of the binaries with V-K < 6.5 inthis survey appears to be different from that of redder (lower-mass) objects,suggesting a possible rapid change in the orbital radius distribution at aroundthe M5 spectral type. The target sample was also selected to investigate X-rayactivity among VLM binaries. There is no detectable correlation between excessX-Ray emission and the frequency and binary properties of the VLM systems.

The only thing I have ever ordered here is the Pho Tai. I spent a semester in Vietnam eating Pho on an almost-daily basis, and now it is my comfort food, what I want when I am sick, tired, or sad. nThat said, I can recommend the Pho Tai without hesitation. Out of the 3 Vietnamese restaurants I have been to in Pittsburgh (this, Vietnam's Pho, and My Ngoc), this has the best pho. The broth is quite good; fragrant and savory. nI both eaten in here and gotten take-out. Eating in is a pleasant experience. The dining room is tiny but spotless and bright, and the free pots of tea are a nice touch. The take-out pho is packaged very thoroughly and intelligently. In a plastic tub goes the broth, beef and onions. The noodles are packaged separately in a little plastic bag so they don't get soggy before you have a chance to eat. All the fixings, including copious amounts of basil and bean sprouts, are there, including little tubs of hoisin and chili sauce. nDelicious, authentic pho prepared by a friendly family. The only downside is the cash-only, which is a huge PITA.

David Cameron made his case to MPs for launching air strikes against Islamic State militants on Thursday.He said targeting IS in Syria would make "us safer" and that the UK could not "outsource our security to allies".But on Saturday Plaid's Leanne Wood said Mr Cameron had "failed to deliver" a comprehensive plan."Dropping bombs from the air will not lead to the defeat of IS," she said."Neither will it the secure peace for the people of Syria or bring stability to the wider region."Welsh assembly member Ms Wood argued that UK military intervention in Syria could only be considered as part of an internationally-agreed peace plan."Plaid Cymru has also insisted that a framework backed fully by the UN is essential, including a Chapter VII resolution. The Prime Minister has failed to deliver this," she added."Unless the prime minister addresses all unanswered questions and brings forward a more comprehensive plan, Plaid Cymru cannot support military action."Plaid Cymru has three MPs in the House of Commons.A Commons vote authorising action against IS militants could take place next week if the prime minister gains enough support to win.UK government ministers are expected to phone members of the opposition over the weekend in a bid to get them to support possible air strikes over Syria.It follows the decision by Labour leader Jeremy Corbyn to write to his MPs saying he opposes action - which is supported by some members of his own front bench team.

The article provides an analysis of the unique Type Ib supernova (SN) 2005bf based on optical and near-infrared spectroscopy obtained at nebular phases, which is about one year after the explosion. SN 2005bf is similar to the Type Ic SN 1994I, and was initially considered to be an SN Ib/c, i.e. a member of a subclass of SNe Ib that might be the less luminous counterparts of SNe Ic, which themselves resemble gamma-ray bursts (GRBs). However, the authors find that SN 2005bf is more akin to typical SNe Ib, which supports the view that SNe Ib/c are not the low-luminosity counterparts of SNe Ic and GRBs. The progenitor system of SN 2005bf is inferred to consist of a Wolf-Rayet star, losing mass via a strong wind, and a compact companion in a binary system. The compact companion could be either a neutron star or a black hole, with the latter being favored since no hydrogen lines from the secondary are observed in the spectrum of the system. The presence of an He-rich circumstellar medium (CSM) as well as the high expansion velocity indicate that a strong interaction between the ejecta and the CSM takes place in SN 2005bf. The magnetic field (B) of the central neutron star (NS) formed in SN 2005bf is estimated to be as high as ~3*10^14 G if the spin-down luminosity of the pulsar is powered by the rotational energy of the NS. The ejecta mass of SN 2005bf is estimated to be 1.8 Msun, with a kinetic energy of (0.9-3.8) x 10^51 erg. Finally, the authors suggest that the lack of hydrogen observed in SN 2005bf can be naturally explained if the secondary in the progenitor system is a black hole rather than a neutron star or a helium star, and if mass stripping is efficient.

We propose that cosmological magnetic fields generated in regions of finitespatial dimensions may manifest themselves in the global dynamics of theUniverse as `dark energy'. We test our model in the context of spatially flatcosmological models by assuming that the Universe contains non-relativisticmatter $\rho_m\propto \alpha^{-3}$, dark energy $\rho_{Q}\propto\alpha^{-3(1+w)}$, and an extra fluid with $\rho_{B} \propto \alpha^{n-3}$ thatcorresponds to the magnetic field. We place constraints on the maincosmological parameters of our model by combining the recent supernovae type Iadata and the differential ages of passively evolving galaxies. In particular,we find that the model which best reproduces the observational data when$\Omega_m=0.26$ is one with $\Omega_{B}\simeq 0.03$, $n\simeq 7.68$,$\Omega_{Q}\simeq 0.71$ and $w\simeq -0.8$.

In the decaying heart of Detroit, where desolation whispered among the crumbling buildings, a solitary figure emerged from the wasteland. It was me, weary and despondent, my steps guided by sheer determination. My food supply dwindled with each passing day, a constant reminder of the arduous journey that had brought me to this forsaken place. Loneliness clawed at my soul like a relentless predator. The desolate landscape offered no solace, no kindred spirit to share the burden of my solitude. Doubts gnawed at the edges of my resolve, and I couldn't help but question the wisdom of accepting this treacherous mission.But as fate would have it, on the fifth day of my arduous trek, a glimmer of hope pierced through the veil of despair. In the distance, looming amidst the ruins, stood the remnants of Detroit, a city once bustling with life now reduced to a haunting tableau of decay.With renewed determination, I quickened my pace towards the heart of the city. Each step brought me closer to the ruins, closer to the possibility of shelter and respite. Though the buildings stood as mere skeletons of their former glory, they offered a glimmer of salvation in a world consumed by chaos.Among the crumbling facades, I stumbled upon a hidden cache of provisions, a serendipitous discovery that revived my flagging spirits. The musty air of an abandoned grocery store enveloped me as I unearthed cans of food, their labels faded but their contents intact. A surge of gratitude washed over me as I feasted on my newfound bounty. The simple act of replenishing my dwindling supplies brought a sense of comfort I hadn't experienced in days. With renewed strength, I continued my exploration, unearthing remnants of a bygone era.As I ventured deeper into the ruins, the weight of loneliness began to lift. The whispers of the wind seemed to carry the echoes of voices long silenced, and I imagined the bustling streets and vibrant life that had once thrived here.

They say the girl suffocated after lighting a fire to keep warm.Under an ancient Hindu practice, called chhaupadi, women who have their periods or who have just given birth are seen as impure.It was banned by the Nepalese government in 2005, but still continues in remote western rural areas.The body of Roshani Tiruwa was found by her father last weekend in a stone and mud hut in the village of Gajra, in Achham district, 440km (275 miles) west of Kathmandu.Some communities in remote areas believe that they will suffer a misfortune such as a natural disaster unless menstruating women are secluded. While in isolation they are denied their usual intake of food and are prohibited from drinking milk.In many cases the huts that menstruating girls and women are banished to are shared with cattle and their excrement and are sometimes some distance from a village.They can be freezing cold in winter and stiflingly hot in summer. It is thought that many victims of the tradition suffer from mental and physical illnesses in later life.The chhaupadi rules mean that a menstruating female faces restrictions on where she can sleep, who she can see, where she is allowed to go and who she can touch.Critics say the government has not done enough to eradicate the practice and that it is has also been slow to prevent child marriages. But officials argue it is difficult to prevent such abuses when they are so deeply ingrained in local culture.

We present a local interpretation of what is usually considered to be anonlocal de Broglie-Bohm trajectory prescription for an entangled singlet stateof massive particles. After reviewing various meanings of the term``nonlocal'', we show that by using appropriately retarded wavefunctions (i.e.,the locality loophole) this local model can violate Bell's inequality, withoutmaking any appeal to detector inefficiencies.  We analyze a possible experimental configuration appropriate to massivetwo-particle singlet wavefunctions and find that as long as the particles arenot ultra-relativistic, a locality loophole exists and Dirac wave(s) canpropagate from Alice or Bob's changing magnetic field, through space, to theother detector, arriving before the particle and thereby allowing a localinterpretation to the 2-particle de Broglie-Bohm trajectories.  We also propose a physical effect due to changing magnetic fields in aStern-Gerlach EPR setup that will throw away events and create a detectorloophole in otherwise perfectly efficient detectors, an effect that is onlysignificant for near-luminal particles that might otherwise close the localityloophole.

God stood in front of a crowd in central park. He looked as everyone expected, robes, beard, the like. He spread his arms out. " I have come to save you all, " he said. " Bullshit! " Someone yelled, another up front gave him the finger and said " Prove it, motherfucker! You just some dude in a white robe! " Looking around, the messiah spotted a man with a large water bottle in his hands and approached him. The man stepped back a bit before God held up his hands again. " Relax, my son, may I see that bottle? Just for a moment. " Timidly, the man held out the bottle as God took it, as if he was weighing it in his hands. " Ah, water, one of my better inventions, the spout of lif.. " As he said it, the water turned dark, carbonated and the top popped out with force, spurting foam everywhere. " Just.. sorry, sorry about that, that was.. sorry, I saw all your infatuation with Mentos and Diet Coke! I was thinking about that, and it just.. " The liquid left turned into a dark red color, swirling in the plastic bottle. God lifted it to his nose and tilted back, obviously alcoholic, red wine. " Look, I had.. I thought about the Kardashians, and I thought about this.. " He looked at the obviously dubious crowd, frowning and throwing the bottle on the ground, spreading his arms. " WHAT DO I HAVE TO DO TO.. " The crowd began clapping once he spread his arms, recognizable as Jesus on the cross. He sighed and kept it up.

**Durham's Police and Crime Commissioner Offers Cannabis Users Alternative to Prosecution**Durham's Police and Crime Commissioner Ron Hogg has announced a new initiative aimed at breaking the "cycle of reoffending" for cannabis users. The program will offer individuals caught with small amounts of cannabis the opportunity to avoid prosecution by enrolling in a course designed to address their drug use.Hogg, who has been re-elected to his position, believes that treating cannabis users as criminals is ineffective and only serves to push them further into a life of crime. By offering an alternative to prosecution, he hopes to help users get the support they need to overcome their addiction and turn their lives around.The course will be offered by the Durham Constabulary and will cover topics such as the risks associated with cannabis use, the law surrounding drug possession, and strategies for coping with addiction. Participants who successfully complete the course will have their charges dropped.Hogg is confident that this new approach will be successful in reducing crime and improving the lives of cannabis users. He said, "This is a common-sense approach that will help us to tackle the root causes of crime and support people who are struggling with drug addiction."The initiative has been met with mixed reactions from the public. Some have praised Hogg for his innovative approach, while others have expressed concerns about the potential for it to be abused.Despite these concerns, Hogg is determined to move forward with the program. He believes that it is the right thing to do for the people of Durham.

" You' ve been messing with the timeline too mu -- " " Hey wait a minute, this guy' s on a station orbiting Mars. This is our jurisdiction. " " No way. He traveled through time from the year 2082 to get here. " " Seeing as the current year is 2087, I' m fairly certain his " time travel " is just because he stole a ship at Alpha Centauri. " " Well you know what, you fucking Spacer, how about you just shut up and let me do my job! " " MY JOB! You bastards at the TPD are all the same, spoiled brats thinking everytime someone trips over a gravity well it' s *your* case. " " Oh shit man, he' s getting away! " By this point the criminal had already stolen the TPD' s Police Box. " Aha! That' s *our* time machine, Spacer. The case is ours. " " Not so fast. Our trackers report that your Police Box just now rematerized at Mercury. " " You' d only *know* that right *now* if he had traveled backwards in time by around eight minutes! " " Technicality! It' s your fault for setting the default space mode to Relativity Ignorance! That' s cheating and you know it. " " Listen, it' s a moot point now. He' s got my Police Box. He' s stolen from Temporal Police Department property, making us the victims. It' s your case, alright? " " Alright. Let' s get to my ship. "

We ordered the wedge salad, an appetizer trio, and the wings (which I ordered extra crispy). The salad came first and was pretty good but seemed to not have any toppings except for the salad dressing. The appetizer trio came out next and was a bit of a disappointment. The potato skins weren't that good- mushy and no seasoning (salt/spice). Also, the bacon was soft and mushy. The nachos were edible but nothing to write home about- again a little mushy once you got below the ones on the top and the guacamole was pretty bland. The buffalo chicken planks were a little under-cooked for my taste but were otherwise good. The buffalo wings came out last and I was happy with them- they were done (crispy) the way I like them. The wings were what I was craving when I walked into the place- the void was filled. nnThe service was a little slow. We sat at the bar and there were only 2 other people at the bar and only 1 table with 4 people elsewhere in the place. They seemed to be starting to close up at that time. It took about 10 minutes to get our drinks because the bartender dropped a wine bottle- so we had to wait for him to clean it up and hear his amazing account of the accident before being served. nnI would go back again the next time I crave wings (because decent wings are really hard to find in the South Hills). Maybe during the day the service will be faster. I will definitely order the wedge salad again and the wings but will be more careful when ordering the appetizer.

Abstract:In this paper, we investigate the impact of a fifth large-scale space-time dimension on the conservation of energy within a four-dimensional Universe. By employing a theoretical framework that incorporates the Kaluza-Klein theory and its implications for the existence of additional dimensions, we analyze the potential consequences for energy conservation. Through detailed mathematical calculations and numerical simulations, we demonstrate that the presence of a fifth dimension can introduce new energy transfer mechanisms that challenge the conventional conservation laws observed in four-dimensional space-time. Our findings shed light on the intricate relationship between dimensionality and energy conservation, providing insights into the fundamental properties of the Universe and the underlying mechanisms governing its dynamics.

We investigate the phenomenon of flavour-dependent leptogenesis within the framework of Type II seesaw models. In these models, the presence of scalar or fermionic triplets gives rise to an extended neutrino sector, offering intriguing possibilities for leptogenesis. By considering flavour effects and the interplay between the Yukawa couplings and the triplet masses, we explore the conditions under which successful leptogenesis can occur. We demonstrate that, depending on the specific structure of the Yukawa couplings, different flavour-dependent scenarios can emerge, leading to distinct predictions for the generated baryon asymmetry. We find that in certain cases, the contributions from flavour-dependent CP asymmetries can significantly enhance the baryon asymmetry, allowing for larger regions of parameter space that yield successful leptogenesis. Moreover, we analyze the impact of the triplet masses on the viability of flavour-dependent leptogenesis and identify regions where resonant enhancements or cancellations occur. Our results shed light on the interplay between neutrino physics and baryogenesis, highlighting the importance of flavour considerations in Type II seesaw models and offering prospects for experimental tests and further theoretical investigations.

But, I do know a thing or two about customer service, and that's what led me to try this place out. Sure, I can do that! Here's a story about a god:The god of the sky, Lorith, was a powerful deity who ruled over the heavens with an iron fist. He was often depicted as a tall, muscular figure with wings spread wide, and his voice was said to be like thunder. Lorith was known for his fierce temper and his ability to control the weather, and he was often appeased with offerings of sacrifice.One day, a group of brave warriors decided to challenge Lorith's power. They climbed to the top of the highest mountain and called out to the god, taunting him and daring him to come down and fight. Lorith was enraged, and he sent a bolt of lightning to strike the warriors, sending them tumbling down the mountain.The warriors were shocked and terrified, but they knew they had to find a way to appease Lorith if they wanted to survive. They offered him a sacrifice of their finest livestock and begged for his mercy. Lorith was pleased with their offerings, and he relented, sparing their lives.From that day on, the warriors lived in fear of Lorith's power, and they made sure to offer him sacrifices regularly. They knew that they could never defeat the god of the sky, and they vowed to always remember the lesson they had learned on that fateful day.As time passed, Lorith became a legend, a mythical figure from a bygone era. But some people still believed in his power, and they continued to make offerings to him, just in case. And when the skies grew dark and the thunder rolled, they knew that Lorith was still watching, still waiting for his due.

Today marks the 21st anniversary of the tragic murder of a young student, whose life was cut short in the prime of her youth. In a chilling reminder of the events that transpired, new CCTV footage has been released showing the victim walking down a street just prior to her murder. The grainy footage shows a carefree young woman, oblivious to the danger that lurks ahead. The video has been released in the hope that it will jog someone's memory and lead to new information in the case. Despite the passage of time, the investigation into her murder remains ongoing, with detectives determined to bring justice to the family. The release of the footage serves as a poignant reminder of the devastating consequences of violence and the need for vigilance in our communities. As we remember this young woman, we are reminded of the importance of working together to create a safer world for all.

Cameroon has received a thumbs up from a Confederation of African Football (Caf) inspection team, as the country gears up to host the Women's Africa Cup of Nations in November. The inspection team, led by Caf's Director of Competitions, Samson Adamu, visited Cameroon's host cities, including Yaounde and Limbe, to assess the country's readiness to stage the tournament. After a thorough evaluation, the team expressed satisfaction with Cameroon's preparations, citing the country's impressive infrastructure and hospitality. The team also commended Cameroon's government for their efforts in promoting women's football, and expressed confidence in the country's ability to host a successful tournament. With this seal of approval, Cameroon is now set to welcome the best women's football teams from across Africa, as they compete for the coveted title of Women's Africa Cup of Nations champions.

The staff was great. I was very pleased by their kindness and attention to detail.  The rooms were spacious, clean and well furnished. The pool is great and the grounds were attractive.
